@@ -0,0 +1,449 @@
+# KEP-83: Workload preemption
+
+<!-- toc -->
+- [Summary](#summary)
+- [Motivation](#motivation)
+  - [Goals](#goals)
+  - [Non-Goals](#non-goals)
+- [Proposal](#proposal)
+  - [User Stories (Optional)](#user-stories-optional)
+    - [Story 1](#story-1)
+    - [Story 2](#story-2)
+  - [Notes/Contraints/Caveats (Optional)](#notescontraintscaveats-optional)
+  - [Risks and Mitigations](#risks-and-mitigations)
+- [Design Details](#design-details)
+  - [Test Plan](#test-plan)
+      - [Prerequisite testing updates](#prerequisite-testing-updates)
+    - [Unit Tests](#unit-tests)
+    - [Integration tests](#integration-tests)
+  - [Graduation Criteria](#graduation-criteria)
+- [Implementation History](#implementation-history)
+- [Drawbacks](#drawbacks)
+- [Alternatives](#alternatives)
+<!-- /toc -->
+
+## Summary
+
+This enhancement introduces workload preemption, a mechanism to terminate
+workloads in ClusterQueues that are borrowing resources from the cohort
+when other ClusterQueues under their minimum quota need the resources.
+
+API fields in the Workload, LocalQueue and ClusterQueue objects control
+different preemption policies.
+
+## Motivation
+
+when ClusterQueues under their minimum quota lend resources, they should
+be able to recover those resources fast, to be able to admit workloads
+when there are sudden spikes.
+
+Currently, the only mechanism to recover those resources is to wait for
+Workloads to finish, which is generally unbounded.
+
+### Goals
+
+- Preempt Workloads from ClusterQueues borrowing resources when other
+  ClusterQueues in the cohort, under their minimum quota, need the resources.
+- Introduce API fields in Workload, LocalQueue and ClusterQueue to inform
+  which Workloads can be preempted and in which order.
+
+### Non-Goals
+
+- Graceful termination of Workloads is left to the controller that integrates
+  with the particular workload API.
+  For example, the integration with Job uses the [suspend field](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/job/#suspending-a-job).
+- Partial workload suspension is not supported.
```

  list preemption within a clusterQueue here if it is not a goal

+# KEP-83: Workload preemption
+
+<!-- toc -->
+- [Summary](#summary)

+    - [Story 2](#story-2)

+  - [Risks and Mitigations](#risks-and-mitigations)
+- [Design Details](#design-details)
+  - [Test Plan](#test-plan)
+      - [Prerequisite testing updates](#prerequisite-testing-updates)
+    - [Unit Tests](#unit-tests)
+    - [Integration tests](#integration-tests)
+  - [Graduation Criteria](#graduation-criteria)
+- [Implementation History](#implementation-history)
+- [Drawbacks](#drawbacks)
+- [Alternatives](#alternatives)
+<!-- /toc -->
+
+## Summary
+
+This enhancement introduces workload preemption, a mechanism to terminate
+workloads in ClusterQueues that are borrowing resources from the cohort
+when other ClusterQueues under their minimum quota need the resources.
+
+API fields in the Workload, LocalQueue and ClusterQueue objects control
+different preemption policies.
+
+## Motivation
+
+when ClusterQueues under their minimum quota lend resources, they should
+be able to recover those resources fast, to be able to admit workloads
+when there are sudden spikes.
+
+Currently, the only mechanism to recover those resources is to wait for
+Workloads to finish, which is generally unbounded.
+
+### Goals
+
+- Preempt Workloads from ClusterQueues borrowing resources when other
+  ClusterQueues in the cohort, under their minimum quota, need the resources.
+- Introduce API fields in Workload, LocalQueue and ClusterQueue to inform
+  which Workloads can be preempted and in which order.
+
+### Non-Goals
+
+- Graceful termination of Workloads is left to the controller that integrates
+  with the particular workload API.
+  For example, the integration with Job uses the [suspend field](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/job/#suspending-a-job).
+- Partial workload suspension is not supported.
+
+## Proposal
+
+<!--
+This is where we get down to the specifics of what the proposal actually is.
+This should have enough detail that reviewers can understand exactly what
+you're proposing, but should not include things like API designs or
+implementation. What is the desired outcome and how do we measure success?.
+The "Design Details" section below is for the real
+nitty-gritty.
+-->
+This enhancement proposes the introduction of the following APIs to influence
+Workload preemption:
+- A field in the Workload to describe the cost of preempting a Workload at a
+  given time.
+- A field in the LocalQueue to set Workload defaults.
+- A field in the ClusterQueue to influence how long to wait before starting
+  preemption.
+
+And an algorithm for selecting a set of Workloads using borrowed quota to be
+preempted when a Workload in a ClusterQueue is under the min quota.
+
+### User Stories (Optional)
+
+<!--
+Detail the things that people will be able to do if this KEP is implemented.
+Include as much detail as possible so that people can understand the "how" of
+the system. The goal here is to make this feel real for users without getting
+bogged down.
+-->
+
+#### Story 1
+
+As a batch user, I know how safe it is to disrupt some workloads after they
+have been running for some time.
+
+An sample Workload has the following characteristics:
```

  ```

@@ -0,0 +1,449 @@
+# KEP-83: Workload preemption
+
+<!-- toc -->
+- [Summary](#summary)
+- [Motivation](#motivation)
+  - [Goals](#goals)
+  - [Non-Goals](#non-goals)
+- [Proposal](#proposal)
+  - [User Stories (Optional)](#user-stories-optional)
+    - [Story 1](#story-1)
+    - [Story 2](#story-2)
+  - [Notes/Contraints/Caveats (Optional)](#notescontraintscaveats-optional)
+  - [Risks and Mitigations](#risks-and-mitigations)
+- [Design Details](#design-details)
+  - [Test Plan](#test-plan)
+      - [Prerequisite testing updates](#prerequisite-testing-updates)
+    - [Unit Tests](#unit-tests)
+    - [Integration tests](#integration-tests)
+  - [Graduation Criteria](#graduation-criteria)
+- [Implementation History](#implementation-history)
+- [Drawbacks](#drawbacks)
+- [Alternatives](#alternatives)
+<!-- /toc -->
+
+## Summary
+
+This enhancement introduces workload preemption, a mechanism to terminate
+workloads in ClusterQueues that are borrowing resources from the cohort
+when other ClusterQueues under their minimum quota need the resources.
+
+API fields in the Workload, LocalQueue and ClusterQueue objects control
+different preemption policies.
+
+## Motivation
+
+when ClusterQueues under their minimum quota lend resources, they should
+be able to recover those resources fast, to be able to admit workloads
+when there are sudden spikes.
+
+Currently, the only mechanism to recover those resources is to wait for
+Workloads to finish, which is generally unbounded.
+
+### Goals
+
+- Preempt Workloads from ClusterQueues borrowing resources when other
+  ClusterQueues in the cohort, under their minimum quota, need the resources.
+- Introduce API fields in Workload, LocalQueue and ClusterQueue to inform
+  which Workloads can be preempted and in which order.
+
+### Non-Goals
+
+- Graceful termination of Workloads is left to the controller that integrates
+  with the particular workload API.
+  For example, the integration with Job uses the [suspend field](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/job/#suspending-a-job).
+- Partial workload suspension is not supported.
+
+## Proposal
+
+<!--
+This is where we get down to the specifics of what the proposal actually is.
+This should have enough detail that reviewers can understand exactly what
+you're proposing, but should not include things like API designs or
+implementation. What is the desired outcome and how do we measure success?.
+The "Design Details" section below is for the real
+nitty-gritty.
+-->
+This enhancement proposes the introduction of the following APIs to influence
+Workload preemption:
+- A field in the Workload to describe the cost of preempting a Workload at a
+  given time.
+- A field in the LocalQueue to set Workload defaults.
+- A field in the ClusterQueue to influence how long to wait before starting
+  preemption.
+
+And an algorithm for selecting a set of Workloads using borrowed quota to be
+preempted when a Workload in a ClusterQueue is under the min quota.
+
+### User Stories (Optional)
+
+<!--
+Detail the things that people will be able to do if this KEP is implemented.
+Include as much detail as possible so that people can understand the "how" of
+the system. The goal here is to make this feel real for users without getting
+bogged down.
+-->
+
+#### Story 1
+
+As a batch user, I know how safe it is to disrupt some workloads after they
+have been running for some time.
+
+An sample Workload has the following characteristics:
+Early in their execution, the Workload hasn't made much progress, so it can be
+preempted. Later, the Worload is on the path of doing significant progress, so
+it's best not to disturb it. Lastly, the Workload is expected to have made
+some checkpoints, so it's ok to disturb it.
+
+This can be expressed with the following configuration:
+
+```yaml
+apiVersion: kueue.x-k8s.io/v1alpha2
+kind: Workload
+metadata:
+  name: my-workload
+spec:
+  preemption:
+    disruptionCostMilestones:
+    - seconds: 60
+      cost: 100
+    - seconds: 600
+      cost: 0
+```
+
+The cost is a linear interpolation of the configuration above. A graphical
+representation of the cost looks like the following (not to scale):
+
+```
+cost
+
+100      __
+        /  \___
+       /       \___
+      /            \___
+0    /                 \_
+    0    60             600  time
+```
+
+As a cluster administrator, I can configure default `disruptionCostMilestones`
+for certain workloads using webhooks or setting them for all Workloads in a
+LocalQueue:
+
+```yaml
+apiVersion: kueue.x-k8s.io/v1alpha2
+kind: LocalQueue
+metadata:
+  name: cluster-total
+spec:
+  preemptionDefaults:
+    disruptionCostMilestones:
+    - seconds: 60
+      cost: 100
+    - seconds: 600
+      cost: 0
+```
+
+#### Story 2
+
+As a cluster administrator, in the cluster I have cohorts with different
+characteristics:
+
+- In some cohorts, all the ClusterQueues are known to have short-running
+  workloads that don't do checkpointing. In this case, it's fine to wait for
+  jobs to finish, but don't allow ClusterQueues to borrow more resources.
+- In other cohorts, ClusterQueues might have long running jobs that support
+  checkpointing. In this case, it's necessary to preempt Workloads that are
+  using borrowed quota.
+
+I want to configure in the ClusterQueue how long to wait for Workloads to finish
+up to a limit.
+
+```yaml
+apiVersion: kueue.x-k8s.io/v1alpha2
+kind: ClusterQueue
+metadata:
+  name: cluster-total
+spec:
+  preemption:
+    waitBeforePreemptionSeconds: 600 # leave empty to wait indefinitely.
```

  In a sense, this value is a mix of how long the jobs are welling to wait and the characteristics of the workloads running in other clusterQueues (for example the frequency of checkpointing of the other clusterQueues jobs). 
  
  I wonder if it will be hard for admins to pick a value in practice since different clusterQueues in the cohort may have different characteristics; to simplify it, we should document practices on how to create cohorts in the context of preemption.

- **keps/83-workload-preemption/README.md:112** by [@ahg-g](https://github.com/ahg-g) — 2022-10-05T17:27:41Z
```diff

+- [Design Details](#design-details)

+
+## Summary
+
+This enhancement introduces workload preemption, a mechanism to terminate
+workloads in ClusterQueues that are borrowing resources from the cohort
+when other ClusterQueues under their minimum quota need the resources.
+
+API fields in the Workload, LocalQueue and ClusterQueue objects control
+different preemption policies.
+
+## Motivation
+
+when ClusterQueues under their minimum quota lend resources, they should
+be able to recover those resources fast, to be able to admit workloads
+when there are sudden spikes.
+
+Currently, the only mechanism to recover those resources is to wait for
+Workloads to finish, which is generally unbounded.
+
+### Goals
+
+- Preempt Workloads from ClusterQueues borrowing resources when other
+  ClusterQueues in the cohort, under their minimum quota, need the resources.
+- Introduce API fields in Workload, LocalQueue and ClusterQueue to inform
+  which Workloads can be preempted and in which order.
+
+### Non-Goals
+
+- Graceful termination of Workloads is left to the controller that integrates
+  with the particular workload API.
+  For example, the integration with Job uses the [suspend field](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/job/#suspending-a-job).
+- Partial workload suspension is not supported.
+
+## Proposal
+
+<!--
+This is where we get down to the specifics of what the proposal actually is.
+This should have enough detail that reviewers can understand exactly what
+you're proposing, but should not include things like API designs or
+implementation. What is the desired outcome and how do we measure success?.
+The "Design Details" section below is for the real
+nitty-gritty.
+-->
+This enhancement proposes the introduction of the following APIs to influence
+Workload preemption:
+- A field in the Workload to describe the cost of preempting a Workload at a
+  given time.
+- A field in the LocalQueue to set Workload defaults.
+- A field in the ClusterQueue to influence how long to wait before starting
+  preemption.
+
+And an algorithm for selecting a set of Workloads using borrowed quota to be
+preempted when a Workload in a ClusterQueue is under the min quota.
+
+### User Stories (Optional)
+
+<!--
+Detail the things that people will be able to do if this KEP is implemented.
+Include as much detail as possible so that people can understand the "how" of
+the system. The goal here is to make this feel real for users without getting
+bogged down.
+-->
+
+#### Story 1
+
+As a batch user, I know how safe it is to disrupt some workloads after they
+have been running for some time.
+
+An sample Workload has the following characteristics:
+Early in their execution, the Workload hasn't made much progress, so it can be
+preempted. Later, the Worload is on the path of doing significant progress, so
+it's best not to disturb it. Lastly, the Workload is expected to have made
+some checkpoints, so it's ok to disturb it.
+
+This can be expressed with the following configuration:
+
+```yaml
+apiVersion: kueue.x-k8s.io/v1alpha2
+kind: Workload
+metadata:
+  name: my-workload
+spec:
+  preemption:
+    disruptionCostMilestones:
+    - seconds: 60
+      cost: 100
+    - seconds: 600
+      cost: 0
+```
```

  At first glance this doesn't look simple. Did we base this API or the general semantics on related batch schedulers? How can we assess or have confidence if this is this what users want or even able to express? 
  
  Also, it is possible that cost is not associated with the run time, should we consider a dynamic approach to setting the cost?

- **keps/83-workload-preemption/README.md:272** by [@ahg-g](https://github.com/ahg-g) — 2022-10-05T17:32:27Z

+

+when ClusterQueues under their minimum quota lend resources, they should

+  For example, the integration with Job uses the [suspend field](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/job/#suspending-a-job).
+- Partial workload suspension is not supported.

+## Proposal
+
+<!--

+-->
+This enhancement proposes the introduction of the following APIs to influence
+Workload preemption:
+- A field in the Workload to describe the cost of preempting a Workload at a
+  given time.
+- A field in the LocalQueue to set Workload defaults.
+- A field in the ClusterQueue to influence how long to wait before starting
+  preemption.
+
+And an algorithm for selecting a set of Workloads using borrowed quota to be
+preempted when a Workload in a ClusterQueue is under the min quota.
+
+### User Stories (Optional)
+
+<!--
+Detail the things that people will be able to do if this KEP is implemented.
+Include as much detail as possible so that people can understand the "how" of
+the system. The goal here is to make this feel real for users without getting
+bogged down.
+-->
+
+#### Story 1
+
+As a batch user, I know how safe it is to disrupt some workloads after they
+have been running for some time.
+
+An sample Workload has the following characteristics:
+Early in their execution, the Workload hasn't made much progress, so it can be
+preempted. Later, the Worload is on the path of doing significant progress, so
+it's best not to disturb it. Lastly, the Workload is expected to have made
+some checkpoints, so it's ok to disturb it.
+
+This can be expressed with the following configuration:
+
+```yaml
+apiVersion: kueue.x-k8s.io/v1alpha2
+kind: Workload
+metadata:
+  name: my-workload
+spec:
+  preemption:
+    disruptionCostMilestones:
+    - seconds: 60
+      cost: 100
+    - seconds: 600
+      cost: 0
+```
+
+The cost is a linear interpolation of the configuration above. A graphical
+representation of the cost looks like the following (not to scale):
+
+```
+cost
+
+100      __
+        /  \___
+       /       \___
+      /            \___
+0    /                 \_
+    0    60             600  time
+```
+
+As a cluster administrator, I can configure default `disruptionCostMilestones`
+for certain workloads using webhooks or setting them for all Workloads in a
+LocalQueue:
+
+```yaml
+apiVersion: kueue.x-k8s.io/v1alpha2
+kind: LocalQueue
+metadata:
+  name: cluster-total
+spec:
+  preemptionDefaults:
+    disruptionCostMilestones:
+    - seconds: 60
+      cost: 100
+    - seconds: 600
+      cost: 0
+```
+
+#### Story 2
+
+As a cluster administrator, in the cluster I have cohorts with different
+characteristics:
+
+- In some cohorts, all the ClusterQueues are known to have short-running
+  workloads that don't do checkpointing. In this case, it's fine to wait for
+  jobs to finish, but don't allow ClusterQueues to borrow more resources.
+- In other cohorts, ClusterQueues might have long running jobs that support
+  checkpointing. In this case, it's necessary to preempt Workloads that are
+  using borrowed quota.
+
+I want to configure in the ClusterQueue how long to wait for Workloads to finish
+up to a limit.
+
+```yaml
+apiVersion: kueue.x-k8s.io/v1alpha2
+kind: ClusterQueue
+metadata:
+  name: cluster-total
+spec:
+  preemption:
+    waitBeforePreemptionSeconds: 600 # leave empty to wait indefinitely.
+```
+
+### Notes/Contraints/Caveats (Optional)
+
+<!--
+What are the caveats to the proposal?
+What are some important details that didn't come across above?
+Go in to as much detail as necessary here.
+This might be a good place to talk about core concepts and how they relate.
+-->
+
+### Risks and Mitigations
+
+<!--
+What are the risks of this proposal, and how do we mitigate? Think broadly.
+For example, consider both security and how this will impact the larger
+Kubernetes ecosystem.
+
+How will security be reviewed, and by whom?
+
+How will UX be reviewed, and by whom?
+
+Consider including folks who also work outside the SIG or subproject.
+-->
+### Increased admission latency
+
+## Design Details
+
+The proposal consists of new API fields and a preemption algorithm.
+
+### API
+
+#### Workload changes
+
+The new API fields in a Workload describe the policies and preferences for
+preempting the Workload.
+
+```golang
+
+const WorkloadStarted = "Started"
+
+type WorkloadSpec struct {
+  ...
+  // preemption describes the policies and preferences for preempting this
+  // Workload.
+  // preemption is immutable once set.
+  Preemption *WorkloadPreemption
+}
+
+type WorklodPreemption struct {
+  // disruptionCostMilestones defines a cost curve for preempting this workload
+  // based on the time the workload has been running.
+  // The curve is defined by points of (time, cost). The cost is calculated from
+  // a linear interpolation of the cost between the two time points that contain
+  // the current workload runtime.
+  // The first point is implicitly (0, 0)
+  // The cost from the last milestone is constant.
+  //
+  // For example: given the points (60, 0) and (120, 100), the cost of
+  // preempting the Workload after it has been running for:
+  // - 30s is 0,
+  // - 90s is 50,
+  // - 300s is 100.
+  //
+  // disruptionCostMilestones[i].time > disruptioncostMilestones[j].time for i > j
+  DisruptionCostMilestones []DisruptionCostMilestone.
+}
+
+type DisruptionCostMilestone struct {
+  // seconds is the time since the Workload started (timestamp of the Started
+  // condition on the Workload).
+  Seconds int64
+
+  // cost is an integer representation of the cost of disrupting the Workload
+  // at a given time. The value has meaning when compared to the cost of
+  // disrupting other Workloads.
+  //
+  // 0 <= cost <= 100
+  Cost int32
+}
+```
+
+#### LocalQueue changes
+
+The new API fields in LocalQueue describe the defaults that apply to Workloads
+in this LocalQueue.
+
+```golang
+type LocalQueueSpec struct {
+  ...
+  // preemptionDefaults are the defaults applied to Workloads assigned to this
+  // LocalQueue. The defauts are applied to Workloads at the time of the
+  // Workload admission.
+  PreemptionDefaults *WorkloadPreemption
+}
+```
+
+#### ClusterQueue changes
+
+The new API fields in ClusterQueue describe how to influence the selection
+of Workloads to preempt.
+
+```golang
+const ClusterQueuePendingWorkloadsUnderMinQuota = "PendingWorkloadsUnderMinQuota"
```

  missing description

- **keps/83-workload-preemption/README.md:221** by [@ahg-g](https://github.com/ahg-g) — 2022-10-05T17:37:38Z
```diff
@@ -0,0 +1,449 @@
+# KEP-83: Workload preemption
+
+<!-- toc -->
+- [Summary](#summary)
+- [Motivation](#motivation)
+  - [Goals](#goals)
+  - [Non-Goals](#non-goals)
+- [Proposal](#proposal)
+  - [User Stories (Optional)](#user-stories-optional)
+    - [Story 1](#story-1)
+    - [Story 2](#story-2)
+  - [Notes/Contraints/Caveats (Optional)](#notescontraintscaveats-optional)
+  - [Risks and Mitigations](#risks-and-mitigations)
+- [Design Details](#design-details)
+  - [Test Plan](#test-plan)
+      - [Prerequisite testing updates](#prerequisite-testing-updates)
+    - [Unit Tests](#unit-tests)
+    - [Integration tests](#integration-tests)
+  - [Graduation Criteria](#graduation-criteria)
+- [Implementation History](#implementation-history)
+- [Drawbacks](#drawbacks)
+- [Alternatives](#alternatives)
+<!-- /toc -->
+
+## Summary
+
+This enhancement introduces workload preemption, a mechanism to terminate
+workloads in ClusterQueues that are borrowing resources from the cohort
+when other ClusterQueues under their minimum quota need the resources.
+
+API fields in the Workload, LocalQueue and ClusterQueue objects control
+different preemption policies.
+
+## Motivation
+
+when ClusterQueues under their minimum quota lend resources, they should
+be able to recover those resources fast, to be able to admit workloads
+when there are sudden spikes.
+
+Currently, the only mechanism to recover those resources is to wait for
+Workloads to finish, which is generally unbounded.
+
+### Goals
+
+- Preempt Workloads from ClusterQueues borrowing resources when other
+  ClusterQueues in the cohort, under their minimum quota, need the resources.
+- Introduce API fields in Workload, LocalQueue and ClusterQueue to inform
+  which Workloads can be preempted and in which order.
+
+### Non-Goals
+
+- Graceful termination of Workloads is left to the controller that integrates
+  with the particular workload API.
+  For example, the integration with Job uses the [suspend field](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/job/#suspending-a-job).
+- Partial workload suspension is not supported.
+
+## Proposal
+
+<!--
+This is where we get down to the specifics of what the proposal actually is.
+This should have enough detail that reviewers can understand exactly what
+you're proposing, but should not include things like API designs or
+implementation. What is the desired outcome and how do we measure success?.
+The "Design Details" section below is for the real
+nitty-gritty.
+-->
+This enhancement proposes the introduction of the following APIs to influence
+Workload preemption:
+- A field in the Workload to describe the cost of preempting a Workload at a
+  given time.
+- A field in the LocalQueue to set Workload defaults.
+- A field in the ClusterQueue to influence how long to wait before starting
+  preemption.
+
+And an algorithm for selecting a set of Workloads using borrowed quota to be
+preempted when a Workload in a ClusterQueue is under the min quota.
+
+### User Stories (Optional)
+
+<!--
+Detail the things that people will be able to do if this KEP is implemented.
+Include as much detail as possible so that people can understand the "how" of
+the system. The goal here is to make this feel real for users without getting
+bogged down.
+-->
+
+#### Story 1
+
+As a batch user, I know how safe it is to disrupt some workloads after they
+have been running for some time.
+
+An sample Workload has the following characteristics:
+Early in their execution, the Workload hasn't made much progress, so it can be
+preempted. Later, the Worload is on the path of doing significant progress, so
+it's best not to disturb it. Lastly, the Workload is expected to have made
+some checkpoints, so it's ok to disturb it.
+
+This can be expressed with the following configuration:
+
+```yaml
+apiVersion: kueue.x-k8s.io/v1alpha2
+kind: Workload
+metadata:
+  name: my-workload
+spec:
+  preemption:
+    disruptionCostMilestones:
+    - seconds: 60
+      cost: 100
+    - seconds: 600
+      cost: 0
+```
+
+The cost is a linear interpolation of the configuration above. A graphical
+representation of the cost looks like the following (not to scale):
+
+```
+cost
+
+100      __
+        /  \___
+       /       \___
+      /            \___
+0    /                 \_
+    0    60             600  time
+```
+
+As a cluster administrator, I can configure default `disruptionCostMilestones`
+for certain workloads using webhooks or setting them for all Workloads in a
+LocalQueue:
+
+```yaml
+apiVersion: kueue.x-k8s.io/v1alpha2
+kind: LocalQueue
+metadata:
+  name: cluster-total
+spec:
+  preemptionDefaults:
+    disruptionCostMilestones:
+    - seconds: 60
+      cost: 100
+    - seconds: 600
+      cost: 0
+```
+
+#### Story 2
+
+As a cluster administrator, in the cluster I have cohorts with different
+characteristics:
+
+- In some cohorts, all the ClusterQueues are known to have short-running
+  workloads that don't do checkpointing. In this case, it's fine to wait for
+  jobs to finish, but don't allow ClusterQueues to borrow more resources.
+- In other cohorts, ClusterQueues might have long running jobs that support
+  checkpointing. In this case, it's necessary to preempt Workloads that are
+  using borrowed quota.
+
+I want to configure in the ClusterQueue how long to wait for Workloads to finish
+up to a limit.
+
+```yaml
+apiVersion: kueue.x-k8s.io/v1alpha2
+kind: ClusterQueue
+metadata:
+  name: cluster-total
+spec:
+  preemption:
+    waitBeforePreemptionSeconds: 600 # leave empty to wait indefinitely.
+```
+
+### Notes/Contraints/Caveats (Optional)
+
+<!--
+What are the caveats to the proposal?
+What are some important details that didn't come across above?
+Go in to as much detail as necessary here.
+This might be a good place to talk about core concepts and how they relate.
+-->
+
+### Risks and Mitigations
+
+<!--
+What are the risks of this proposal, and how do we mitigate? Think broadly.
+For example, consider both security and how this will impact the larger
+Kubernetes ecosystem.
+
+How will security be reviewed, and by whom?
+
+How will UX be reviewed, and by whom?
+
+Consider including folks who also work outside the SIG or subproject.
+-->
+### Increased admission latency
+
+## Design Details
+
+The proposal consists of new API fields and a preemption algorithm.
+
+### API
+
+#### Workload changes
+
+The new API fields in a Workload describe the policies and preferences for
+preempting the Workload.
+
+```golang
+
+const WorkloadStarted = "Started"
+
+type WorkloadSpec struct {
+  ...
+  // preemption describes the policies and preferences for preempting this
+  // Workload.
+  // preemption is immutable once set.
+  Preemption *WorkloadPreemption
+}
+
+type WorklodPreemption struct {
+  // disruptionCostMilestones defines a cost curve for preempting this workload
+  // based on the time the workload has been running.
+  // The curve is defined by points of (time, cost). The cost is calculated from
+  // a linear interpolation of the cost between the two time points that contain
+  // the current workload runtime.
+  // The first point is implicitly (0, 0)
+  // The cost from the last milestone is constant.
+  //
+  // For example: given the points (60, 0) and (120, 100), the cost of
+  // preempting the Workload after it has been running for:
+  // - 30s is 0,
+  // - 90s is 50,
+  // - 300s is 100.
+  //
+  // disruptionCostMilestones[i].time > disruptioncostMilestones[j].time for i > j
+  DisruptionCostMilestones []DisruptionCostMilestone.
+}
+
+type DisruptionCostMilestone struct {
+  // seconds is the time since the Workload started (timestamp of the Started
+  // condition on the Workload).
+  Seconds int64
+
+  // cost is an integer representation of the cost of disrupting the Workload
+  // at a given time. The value has meaning when compared to the cost of
+  // disrupting other Workloads.
+  //
+  // 0 <= cost <= 100
+  Cost int32
+}
+```
+
+#### LocalQueue changes
+
+The new API fields in LocalQueue describe the defaults that apply to Workloads
+in this LocalQueue.
+
+```golang
+type LocalQueueSpec struct {
+  ...
+  // preemptionDefaults are the defaults applied to Workloads assigned to this
+  // LocalQueue. The defauts are applied to Workloads at the time of the
+  // Workload admission.
+  PreemptionDefaults *WorkloadPreemption
+}
+```
+
+#### ClusterQueue changes
+
+The new API fields in ClusterQueue describe how to influence the selection
+of Workloads to preempt.
+
+```golang
+const ClusterQueuePendingWorkloadsUnderMinQuota = "PendingWorkloadsUnderMinQuota"
+
+type ClusterQueueSpec struct {
+  ...
+  // preemption describes policies to preempt Workloads from ClusterQueues
+  // borrowing resources when this ClusterQueue has pending Workloads and the
+  // min quota is unmet.
+  preemption ClusterQueuePreemption
+}
+
+type ClusterQueueStatus struct {
+  ...
+  conditions []metav1.Condition
+}
+
+type ClusterQueuePreemption struct {
```

+
+## Summary
+
+This enhancement introduces workload preemption, a mechanism to terminate
+workloads in ClusterQueues that are borrowing resources from the cohort
+when other ClusterQueues under their minimum quota need the resources.
+
+API fields in the Workload, LocalQueue and ClusterQueue objects control
+different preemption policies.
+
+## Motivation
+
+when ClusterQueues under their minimum quota lend resources, they should
+be able to recover those resources fast, to be able to admit workloads
+when there are sudden spikes.
+
+Currently, the only mechanism to recover those resources is to wait for
+Workloads to finish, which is generally unbounded.
+
+### Goals
+
+- Preempt Workloads from ClusterQueues borrowing resources when other
+  ClusterQueues in the cohort, under their minimum quota, need the resources.
+- Introduce API fields in Workload, LocalQueue and ClusterQueue to inform
+  which Workloads can be preempted and in which order.
+
+### Non-Goals
+
+- Graceful termination of Workloads is left to the controller that integrates
+  with the particular workload API.
+  For example, the integration with Job uses the [suspend field](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/job/#suspending-a-job).
+- Partial workload suspension is not supported.
+
+## Proposal
+
+<!--
+This is where we get down to the specifics of what the proposal actually is.
+This should have enough detail that reviewers can understand exactly what
+you're proposing, but should not include things like API designs or
+implementation. What is the desired outcome and how do we measure success?.
+The "Design Details" section below is for the real
+nitty-gritty.
+-->
+This enhancement proposes the introduction of the following APIs to influence
+Workload preemption:
+- A field in the Workload to describe the cost of preempting a Workload at a
+  given time.
+- A field in the LocalQueue to set Workload defaults.
+- A field in the ClusterQueue to influence how long to wait before starting
+  preemption.
+
+And an algorithm for selecting a set of Workloads using borrowed quota to be
+preempted when a Workload in a ClusterQueue is under the min quota.
+
+### User Stories (Optional)
+
+<!--
+Detail the things that people will be able to do if this KEP is implemented.
+Include as much detail as possible so that people can understand the "how" of
+the system. The goal here is to make this feel real for users without getting
+bogged down.
+-->
+
+#### Story 1
+
+As a batch user, I know how safe it is to disrupt some workloads after they
+have been running for some time.
+
+An sample Workload has the following characteristics:
+Early in their execution, the Workload hasn't made much progress, so it can be
+preempted. Later, the Worload is on the path of doing significant progress, so
+it's best not to disturb it. Lastly, the Workload is expected to have made
+some checkpoints, so it's ok to disturb it.
+
+This can be expressed with the following configuration:
+
+```yaml
+apiVersion: kueue.x-k8s.io/v1alpha2
+kind: Workload
+metadata:
+  name: my-workload
+spec:
+  preemption:
+    disruptionCostMilestones:
+    - seconds: 60
+      cost: 100
+    - seconds: 600
+      cost: 0
+```
+
+The cost is a linear interpolation of the configuration above. A graphical
+representation of the cost looks like the following (not to scale):
+
+```
+cost
+
+100      __
+        /  \___
+       /       \___
+      /            \___
+0    /                 \_
+    0    60             600  time
+```
+
+As a cluster administrator, I can configure default `disruptionCostMilestones`
+for certain workloads using webhooks or setting them for all Workloads in a
+LocalQueue:
+
+```yaml
+apiVersion: kueue.x-k8s.io/v1alpha2
+kind: LocalQueue
+metadata:
+  name: cluster-total
+spec:
+  preemptionDefaults:
+    disruptionCostMilestones:
+    - seconds: 60
+      cost: 100
+    - seconds: 600
+      cost: 0
+```
+
+#### Story 2
+
+As a cluster administrator, in the cluster I have cohorts with different
+characteristics:
+
+- In some cohorts, all the ClusterQueues are known to have short-running
+  workloads that don't do checkpointing. In this case, it's fine to wait for
+  jobs to finish, but don't allow ClusterQueues to borrow more resources.
+- In other cohorts, ClusterQueues might have long running jobs that support
+  checkpointing. In this case, it's necessary to preempt Workloads that are
+  using borrowed quota.
+
+I want to configure in the ClusterQueue how long to wait for Workloads to finish
+up to a limit.
+
+```yaml
+apiVersion: kueue.x-k8s.io/v1alpha2
+kind: ClusterQueue
+metadata:
+  name: cluster-total
+spec:
+  preemption:
+    waitBeforePreemptionSeconds: 600 # leave empty to wait indefinitely.
+```
+
+### Notes/Contraints/Caveats (Optional)
+
+<!--
+What are the caveats to the proposal?
+What are some important details that didn't come across above?
+Go in to as much detail as necessary here.
+This might be a good place to talk about core concepts and how they relate.
+-->
+
+### Risks and Mitigations
+
+<!--
+What are the risks of this proposal, and how do we mitigate? Think broadly.
+For example, consider both security and how this will impact the larger
+Kubernetes ecosystem.
+
+How will security be reviewed, and by whom?
+
+How will UX be reviewed, and by whom?
+
+Consider including folks who also work outside the SIG or subproject.
+-->
+### Increased admission latency
+
+## Design Details
+
+The proposal consists of new API fields and a preemption algorithm.
+
+### API
+
+#### Workload changes
+
+The new API fields in a Workload describe the policies and preferences for
+preempting the Workload.
+
+```golang
+
+const WorkloadStarted = "Started"
+
+type WorkloadSpec struct {
+  ...
+  // preemption describes the policies and preferences for preempting this
+  // Workload.
+  // preemption is immutable once set.
+  Preemption *WorkloadPreemption
+}
+
+type WorklodPreemption struct {
+  // disruptionCostMilestones defines a cost curve for preempting this workload
+  // based on the time the workload has been running.
+  // The curve is defined by points of (time, cost). The cost is calculated from
+  // a linear interpolation of the cost between the two time points that contain
+  // the current workload runtime.
+  // The first point is implicitly (0, 0)
+  // The cost from the last milestone is constant.
+  //
+  // For example: given the points (60, 0) and (120, 100), the cost of
+  // preempting the Workload after it has been running for:
+  // - 30s is 0,
+  // - 90s is 50,
+  // - 300s is 100.
+  //
+  // disruptionCostMilestones[i].time > disruptioncostMilestones[j].time for i > j
+  DisruptionCostMilestones []DisruptionCostMilestone.
+}
+
+type DisruptionCostMilestone struct {
+  // seconds is the time since the Workload started (timestamp of the Started
+  // condition on the Workload).
+  Seconds int64
+
+  // cost is an integer representation of the cost of disrupting the Workload
+  // at a given time. The value has meaning when compared to the cost of
+  // disrupting other Workloads.
+  //
+  // 0 <= cost <= 100
+  Cost int32
+}
+```
+
+#### LocalQueue changes
+
+The new API fields in LocalQueue describe the defaults that apply to Workloads
+in this LocalQueue.
+
+```golang
+type LocalQueueSpec struct {
+  ...
+  // preemptionDefaults are the defaults applied to Workloads assigned to this
+  // LocalQueue. The defauts are applied to Workloads at the time of the
+  // Workload admission.
+  PreemptionDefaults *WorkloadPreemption
+}
+```
+
+#### ClusterQueue changes
+
+The new API fields in ClusterQueue describe how to influence the selection
+of Workloads to preempt.
+
+```golang
+const ClusterQueuePendingWorkloadsUnderMinQuota = "PendingWorkloadsUnderMinQuota"
+
+type ClusterQueueSpec struct {
+  ...
+  // preemption describes policies to preempt Workloads from ClusterQueues
+  // borrowing resources when this ClusterQueue has pending Workloads and the
+  // min quota is unmet.
+  preemption ClusterQueuePreemption
+}
+
+type ClusterQueueStatus struct {
+  ...
+  conditions []metav1.Condition
+}
+
+type ClusterQueuePreemption struct {
+  // waitBeforePreemptionSeconds is the time in seconds that the ClusterQueue
+  // is willing to wait for Workloads to finish before issuing preemptions,
+  // since the first time that the ClusterQueue has pending workloads that would
+  // fit under the min quota. This time is present in the timestamp for the
+  // PendingWorkloadsUnderMinQuota condition.
+  // If null, it means to wait indefinitely.
+  WaitBeforePreemptionSeconds *int64
+
+  // <UNRESOLVED>
+
+  // allowPreemptionByLowerPriority determines whether pending Workloads can
+  // preempt Workloads with higher priority in this ClusterQueue.
```

  ```suggestion
    // preempt Workloads with lower priority in this ClusterQueue.
  ```

- **keps/83-workload-preemption/README.md:327** by [@ahg-g](https://github.com/ahg-g) — 2022-10-05T17:44:15Z
```diff
@@ -0,0 +1,449 @@
+# KEP-83: Workload preemption
+
+<!-- toc -->
+- [Summary](#summary)
+- [Motivation](#motivation)
+  - [Goals](#goals)
+  - [Non-Goals](#non-goals)
+- [Proposal](#proposal)
+  - [User Stories (Optional)](#user-stories-optional)
+    - [Story 1](#story-1)
+    - [Story 2](#story-2)
+  - [Notes/Contraints/Caveats (Optional)](#notescontraintscaveats-optional)
+  - [Risks and Mitigations](#risks-and-mitigations)
+- [Design Details](#design-details)
+  - [Test Plan](#test-plan)
+      - [Prerequisite testing updates](#prerequisite-testing-updates)
+    - [Unit Tests](#unit-tests)
+    - [Integration tests](#integration-tests)
+  - [Graduation Criteria](#graduation-criteria)
+- [Implementation History](#implementation-history)
+- [Drawbacks](#drawbacks)
+- [Alternatives](#alternatives)
+<!-- /toc -->
+
+## Summary
+
+This enhancement introduces workload preemption, a mechanism to terminate
+workloads in ClusterQueues that are borrowing resources from the cohort
+when other ClusterQueues under their minimum quota need the resources.
+
+API fields in the Workload, LocalQueue and ClusterQueue objects control
+different preemption policies.
+
+## Motivation
+
+when ClusterQueues under their minimum quota lend resources, they should
+be able to recover those resources fast, to be able to admit workloads
+when there are sudden spikes.
+
+Currently, the only mechanism to recover those resources is to wait for
+Workloads to finish, which is generally unbounded.
+
+### Goals
+
+- Preempt Workloads from ClusterQueues borrowing resources when other
+  ClusterQueues in the cohort, under their minimum quota, need the resources.
+- Introduce API fields in Workload, LocalQueue and ClusterQueue to inform
+  which Workloads can be preempted and in which order.
+
+### Non-Goals
+
+- Graceful termination of Workloads is left to the controller that integrates
+  with the particular workload API.
+  For example, the integration with Job uses the [suspend field](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/job/#suspending-a-job).
+- Partial workload suspension is not supported.
+
+## Proposal
+
+<!--
+This is where we get down to the specifics of what the proposal actually is.
+This should have enough detail that reviewers can understand exactly what
+you're proposing, but should not include things like API designs or
+implementation. What is the desired outcome and how do we measure success?.
+The "Design Details" section below is for the real
+nitty-gritty.
+-->
+This enhancement proposes the introduction of the following APIs to influence
+Workload preemption:
+- A field in the Workload to describe the cost of preempting a Workload at a
+  given time.
+- A field in the LocalQueue to set Workload defaults.
+- A field in the ClusterQueue to influence how long to wait before starting
+  preemption.
+
+And an algorithm for selecting a set of Workloads using borrowed quota to be
+preempted when a Workload in a ClusterQueue is under the min quota.
+
+### User Stories (Optional)
+
+<!--
+Detail the things that people will be able to do if this KEP is implemented.
+Include as much detail as possible so that people can understand the "how" of
+the system. The goal here is to make this feel real for users without getting
+bogged down.
+-->
+
+#### Story 1
+
+As a batch user, I know how safe it is to disrupt some workloads after they
+have been running for some time.
+
+An sample Workload has the following characteristics:
+Early in their execution, the Workload hasn't made much progress, so it can be
+preempted. Later, the Worload is on the path of doing significant progress, so
+it's best not to disturb it. Lastly, the Workload is expected to have made
+some checkpoints, so it's ok to disturb it.
+
+This can be expressed with the following configuration:
+
+```yaml
+apiVersion: kueue.x-k8s.io/v1alpha2
+kind: Workload
+metadata:
+  name: my-workload
+spec:
+  preemption:
+    disruptionCostMilestones:
+    - seconds: 60
+      cost: 100
+    - seconds: 600
+      cost: 0
+```
+
+The cost is a linear interpolation of the configuration above. A graphical
+representation of the cost looks like the following (not to scale):
+
+```
+cost
+
+100      __
+        /  \___
+       /       \___
+      /            \___
+0    /                 \_
+    0    60             600  time
+```
+
+As a cluster administrator, I can configure default `disruptionCostMilestones`
+for certain workloads using webhooks or setting them for all Workloads in a
+LocalQueue:
+
+```yaml
+apiVersion: kueue.x-k8s.io/v1alpha2
+kind: LocalQueue
+metadata:
+  name: cluster-total
+spec:
+  preemptionDefaults:
+    disruptionCostMilestones:
+    - seconds: 60
+      cost: 100
+    - seconds: 600
+      cost: 0
+```
+
+#### Story 2
+
+As a cluster administrator, in the cluster I have cohorts with different
+characteristics:
+
+- In some cohorts, all the ClusterQueues are known to have short-running
+  workloads that don't do checkpointing. In this case, it's fine to wait for
+  jobs to finish, but don't allow ClusterQueues to borrow more resources.
+- In other cohorts, ClusterQueues might have long running jobs that support
+  checkpointing. In this case, it's necessary to preempt Workloads that are
+  using borrowed quota.
+
+I want to configure in the ClusterQueue how long to wait for Workloads to finish
+up to a limit.
+
+```yaml
+apiVersion: kueue.x-k8s.io/v1alpha2
+kind: ClusterQueue
+metadata:
+  name: cluster-total
+spec:
+  preemption:
+    waitBeforePreemptionSeconds: 600 # leave empty to wait indefinitely.
+```
+
+### Notes/Contraints/Caveats (Optional)
+
+<!--
+What are the caveats to the proposal?
+What are some important details that didn't come across above?
+Go in to as much detail as necessary here.
+This might be a good place to talk about core concepts and how they relate.
+-->
+
+### Risks and Mitigations
+
+<!--
+What are the risks of this proposal, and how do we mitigate? Think broadly.
+For example, consider both security and how this will impact the larger
+Kubernetes ecosystem.
+
+How will security be reviewed, and by whom?
+
+How will UX be reviewed, and by whom?
+
+Consider including folks who also work outside the SIG or subproject.
+-->
+### Increased admission latency
+
+## Design Details
+
+The proposal consists of new API fields and a preemption algorithm.
+
+### API
+
+#### Workload changes
+
+The new API fields in a Workload describe the policies and preferences for
+preempting the Workload.
+
+```golang
+
+const WorkloadStarted = "Started"
+
+type WorkloadSpec struct {
+  ...
+  // preemption describes the policies and preferences for preempting this
+  // Workload.
+  // preemption is immutable once set.
+  Preemption *WorkloadPreemption
+}
+
+type WorklodPreemption struct {
+  // disruptionCostMilestones defines a cost curve for preempting this workload
+  // based on the time the workload has been running.
+  // The curve is defined by points of (time, cost). The cost is calculated from
+  // a linear interpolation of the cost between the two time points that contain
+  // the current workload runtime.
+  // The first point is implicitly (0, 0)
+  // The cost from the last milestone is constant.
+  //
+  // For example: given the points (60, 0) and (120, 100), the cost of
+  // preempting the Workload after it has been running for:
+  // - 30s is 0,
+  // - 90s is 50,
+  // - 300s is 100.
+  //
+  // disruptionCostMilestones[i].time > disruptioncostMilestones[j].time for i > j
+  DisruptionCostMilestones []DisruptionCostMilestone.
+}
+
+type DisruptionCostMilestone struct {
+  // seconds is the time since the Workload started (timestamp of the Started
+  // condition on the Workload).
+  Seconds int64
+
+  // cost is an integer representation of the cost of disrupting the Workload
+  // at a given time. The value has meaning when compared to the cost of
+  // disrupting other Workloads.
+  //
+  // 0 <= cost <= 100
+  Cost int32
+}
+```
+
+#### LocalQueue changes
+
+The new API fields in LocalQueue describe the defaults that apply to Workloads
+in this LocalQueue.
+
+```golang
+type LocalQueueSpec struct {
+  ...
+  // preemptionDefaults are the defaults applied to Workloads assigned to this
+  // LocalQueue. The defauts are applied to Workloads at the time of the
+  // Workload admission.
+  PreemptionDefaults *WorkloadPreemption
+}
+```
+
+#### ClusterQueue changes
+
+The new API fields in ClusterQueue describe how to influence the selection
+of Workloads to preempt.
+
+```golang
+const ClusterQueuePendingWorkloadsUnderMinQuota = "PendingWorkloadsUnderMinQuota"
+
+type ClusterQueueSpec struct {
+  ...
+  // preemption describes policies to preempt Workloads from ClusterQueues
+  // borrowing resources when this ClusterQueue has pending Workloads and the
+  // min quota is unmet.
+  preemption ClusterQueuePreemption
+}
+
+type ClusterQueueStatus struct {
+  ...
+  conditions []metav1.Condition
+}
+
+type ClusterQueuePreemption struct {
+  // waitBeforePreemptionSeconds is the time in seconds that the ClusterQueue
+  // is willing to wait for Workloads to finish before issuing preemptions,
+  // since the first time that the ClusterQueue has pending workloads that would
+  // fit under the min quota. This time is present in the timestamp for the
+  // PendingWorkloadsUnderMinQuota condition.
+  // If null, it means to wait indefinitely.
+  WaitBeforePreemptionSeconds *int64
+
+  // <UNRESOLVED>
+
+  // allowPreemptionByLowerPriority determines whether pending Workloads can
+  // preempt Workloads with higher priority in this ClusterQueue.
+  // Defaults to false.
+  AllowPreemptionByLowerPriority bool
+
+  // workloadSorting determines how Workloads from the cohort that are
+  // candidates for preemption are sorted.
+  // Sorting happens at the time when a Workload in this ClusterQueue is
+  // evaluated for admission. All the Workloads in the cohort are sorted based
+  // on the criteria defined in the preempting ClusterQueue.
+  // workloadOrder is a list of comparison criteria between two Workloads that
+  // are evaluated in order.
+  // Possible criteria are:
+  // - ByLowestPriority: Prefer to preempt the Workload with lower priority.
+  // - ByLowestCost: Prefer to preempt the workload with lower preemption cost.
+  // - ByLowestRuntime: Prefer to preempt the Workload that started more
+  //   recently.
+  // - ByLongestRuntime: Prefer to preempt the Workload that started earlier.
+  //
+  // If empty, the behavior is equivalent to
+  // [ByLowestPriority, ByLowestCost, ByLowestRuntime].
+  WorkloadSorting []WorkloadComparison
+
+  type WorkloadSortingCriteria string
+
+  const (
+    ComparisonByLowestPriority = "ByLowestPriority"
+    ComparisonByLowestCost = "ByLowestCost"
+    ComparisonByLowestRuntime = "ByLowestRuntime"
+  )
```

  I don't think this is necessary, at least not initially. The default of priority -> cost -> runtime looks very reasonable and for me it matches the semantics of priority and cost.

+## Summary
+
+This enhancement introduces workload preemption, a mechanism to terminate
+workloads in ClusterQueues that are borrowing resources from the cohort
+when other ClusterQueues under their minimum quota need the resources.
+
+API fields in the Workload, LocalQueue and ClusterQueue objects control
+different preemption policies.
+
+## Motivation
+
+when ClusterQueues under their minimum quota lend resources, they should
+be able to recover those resources fast, to be able to admit workloads
+when there are sudden spikes.
+
+Currently, the only mechanism to recover those resources is to wait for
+Workloads to finish, which is generally unbounded.
+
+### Goals
+
+- Preempt Workloads from ClusterQueues borrowing resources when other
+  ClusterQueues in the cohort, under their minimum quota, need the resources.
+- Introduce API fields in Workload, LocalQueue and ClusterQueue to inform
+  which Workloads can be preempted and in which order.
+
+### Non-Goals
+
+- Graceful termination of Workloads is left to the controller that integrates
+  with the particular workload API.
+  For example, the integration with Job uses the [suspend field](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/job/#suspending-a-job).
+- Partial workload suspension is not supported.
+
+## Proposal
+
+<!--
+This is where we get down to the specifics of what the proposal actually is.
+This should have enough detail that reviewers can understand exactly what
+you're proposing, but should not include things like API designs or
+implementation. What is the desired outcome and how do we measure success?.
+The "Design Details" section below is for the real
+nitty-gritty.
+-->
+This enhancement proposes the introduction of the following APIs to influence
+Workload preemption:
+- A field in the Workload to describe the cost of preempting a Workload at a
+  given time.
+- A field in the LocalQueue to set Workload defaults.
+- A field in the ClusterQueue to influence how long to wait before starting
+  preemption.
+
+And an algorithm for selecting a set of Workloads using borrowed quota to be
+preempted when a Workload in a ClusterQueue is under the min quota.
+
+### User Stories (Optional)
+
+<!--
+Detail the things that people will be able to do if this KEP is implemented.
+Include as much detail as possible so that people can understand the "how" of
+the system. The goal here is to make this feel real for users without getting
+bogged down.
+-->
+
+#### Story 1
+
+As a batch user, I know how safe it is to disrupt some workloads after they
+have been running for some time.
+
+An sample Workload has the following characteristics:
+Early in their execution, the Workload hasn't made much progress, so it can be
+preempted. Later, the Worload is on the path of doing significant progress, so
+it's best not to disturb it. Lastly, the Workload is expected to have made
+some checkpoints, so it's ok to disturb it.
+
+This can be expressed with the following configuration:
+
+```yaml
+apiVersion: kueue.x-k8s.io/v1alpha2
+kind: Workload
+metadata:
+  name: my-workload
+spec:
+  preemption:
+    disruptionCostMilestones:
+    - seconds: 60
+      cost: 100
+    - seconds: 600
+      cost: 0
+```
```

  As far as I could see, existing batch schedulers have a fixed policy to sort workloads or they provide the knob to sort by oldest/youngest or priority.
  
  > How can we assess or have confidence if this is this what users want or even able to express?
  
  My hope is that we can get this feedback by sharing the proposal early. But this is mostly based on the conversation in #83, but more flexible than a plain threshold.
  
  > Also, it is possible that cost is not associated with the run time, should we consider a dynamic approach to setting the cost?
  
  We could add a field in the Workload to set cost (similarly to [pod deletion cost](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/replicaset/#pod-deletion-cost)), but this is much harder to use, as you have to setup a controller to do the updates or give permissions to the job pods to update the Workload.

+- [Drawbacks](#drawbacks)
+- [Alternatives](#alternatives)
+<!-- /toc -->
+
+## Summary
+
+This enhancement introduces workload preemption, a mechanism to terminate
+workloads in ClusterQueues that are borrowing resources from the cohort
+when other ClusterQueues under their minimum quota need the resources.
+
+API fields in the Workload, LocalQueue and ClusterQueue objects control
+different preemption policies.
+
+## Motivation
+
+when ClusterQueues under their minimum quota lend resources, they should
+be able to recover those resources fast, to be able to admit workloads
+when there are sudden spikes.
+
+Currently, the only mechanism to recover those resources is to wait for
+Workloads to finish, which is generally unbounded.
+
+### Goals
+
+- Preempt Workloads from ClusterQueues borrowing resources when other
+  ClusterQueues in the cohort, under their minimum quota, need the resources.
+- Introduce API fields in Workload, LocalQueue and ClusterQueue to inform
+  which Workloads can be preempted and in which order.
+
+### Non-Goals
+
+- Graceful termination of Workloads is left to the controller that integrates
+  with the particular workload API.
+  For example, the integration with Job uses the [suspend field](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/job/#suspending-a-job).
+- Partial workload suspension is not supported.
+
+## Proposal
+
+<!--
+This is where we get down to the specifics of what the proposal actually is.
+This should have enough detail that reviewers can understand exactly what
+you're proposing, but should not include things like API designs or
+implementation. What is the desired outcome and how do we measure success?.
+The "Design Details" section below is for the real
+nitty-gritty.
+-->
+This enhancement proposes the introduction of the following APIs to influence
+Workload preemption:
+- A field in the Workload to describe the cost of preempting a Workload at a
+  given time.
+- A field in the LocalQueue to set Workload defaults.
+- A field in the ClusterQueue to influence how long to wait before starting
+  preemption.
+
+And an algorithm for selecting a set of Workloads using borrowed quota to be
+preempted when a Workload in a ClusterQueue is under the min quota.
+
+### User Stories (Optional)
+
+<!--
+Detail the things that people will be able to do if this KEP is implemented.
+Include as much detail as possible so that people can understand the "how" of
+the system. The goal here is to make this feel real for users without getting
+bogged down.
+-->
+
+#### Story 1
+
+As a batch user, I know how safe it is to disrupt some workloads after they
+have been running for some time.
+
+An sample Workload has the following characteristics:
+Early in their execution, the Workload hasn't made much progress, so it can be
+preempted. Later, the Worload is on the path of doing significant progress, so
+it's best not to disturb it. Lastly, the Workload is expected to have made
+some checkpoints, so it's ok to disturb it.
+
+This can be expressed with the following configuration:
+
+```yaml
+apiVersion: kueue.x-k8s.io/v1alpha2
+kind: Workload
+metadata:
+  name: my-workload
+spec:
+  preemption:
+    disruptionCostMilestones:
+    - seconds: 60
+      cost: 100
+    - seconds: 600
+      cost: 0
+```
+
+The cost is a linear interpolation of the configuration above. A graphical
+representation of the cost looks like the following (not to scale):
+
+```
+cost
+
+100      __
+        /  \___
+       /       \___
+      /            \___
+0    /                 \_
+    0    60             600  time
+```
+
+As a cluster administrator, I can configure default `disruptionCostMilestones`
+for certain workloads using webhooks or setting them for all Workloads in a
+LocalQueue:
+
+```yaml
+apiVersion: kueue.x-k8s.io/v1alpha2
+kind: LocalQueue
+metadata:
+  name: cluster-total
+spec:
+  preemptionDefaults:
+    disruptionCostMilestones:
+    - seconds: 60
+      cost: 100
+    - seconds: 600
+      cost: 0
+```
+
+#### Story 2
+
+As a cluster administrator, in the cluster I have cohorts with different
+characteristics:
+
+- In some cohorts, all the ClusterQueues are known to have short-running
+  workloads that don't do checkpointing. In this case, it's fine to wait for
+  jobs to finish, but don't allow ClusterQueues to borrow more resources.
+- In other cohorts, ClusterQueues might have long running jobs that support
+  checkpointing. In this case, it's necessary to preempt Workloads that are
+  using borrowed quota.
+
+I want to configure in the ClusterQueue how long to wait for Workloads to finish
+up to a limit.
+
+```yaml
+apiVersion: kueue.x-k8s.io/v1alpha2
+kind: ClusterQueue
+metadata:
+  name: cluster-total
+spec:
+  preemption:
+    waitBeforePreemptionSeconds: 600 # leave empty to wait indefinitely.
```

  Added to notes with some thoughts on how to configure it.

- **keps/83-workload-preemption/README.md:272** by [@alculquicondor](https://github.com/alculquicondor) — 2022-10-07T13:53:40Z
```diff
@@ -0,0 +1,449 @@
+# KEP-83: Workload preemption
+
+<!-- toc -->
+- [Summary](#summary)
+- [Motivation](#motivation)
+  - [Goals](#goals)
+  - [Non-Goals](#non-goals)
+- [Proposal](#proposal)
+  - [User Stories (Optional)](#user-stories-optional)
+    - [Story 1](#story-1)
+    - [Story 2](#story-2)
+  - [Notes/Contraints/Caveats (Optional)](#notescontraintscaveats-optional)
+  - [Risks and Mitigations](#risks-and-mitigations)
+- [Design Details](#design-details)
+  - [Test Plan](#test-plan)
+      - [Prerequisite testing updates](#prerequisite-testing-updates)
+    - [Unit Tests](#unit-tests)
+    - [Integration tests](#integration-tests)
+  - [Graduation Criteria](#graduation-criteria)
+- [Implementation History](#implementation-history)
+- [Drawbacks](#drawbacks)
+- [Alternatives](#alternatives)
+<!-- /toc -->
+
+## Summary
+
+This enhancement introduces workload preemption, a mechanism to terminate
+workloads in ClusterQueues that are borrowing resources from the cohort
+when other ClusterQueues under their minimum quota need the resources.
+
+API fields in the Workload, LocalQueue and ClusterQueue objects control
+different preemption policies.
+
+## Motivation
+
+when ClusterQueues under their minimum quota lend resources, they should
+be able to recover those resources fast, to be able to admit workloads
+when there are sudden spikes.
+
+Currently, the only mechanism to recover those resources is to wait for
+Workloads to finish, which is generally unbounded.
+
+### Goals
+
+- Preempt Workloads from ClusterQueues borrowing resources when other
+  ClusterQueues in the cohort, under their minimum quota, need the resources.
+- Introduce API fields in Workload, LocalQueue and ClusterQueue to inform
+  which Workloads can be preempted and in which order.
+
+### Non-Goals
+
+- Graceful termination of Workloads is left to the controller that integrates
+  with the particular workload API.
+  For example, the integration with Job uses the [suspend field](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/job/#suspending-a-job).
+- Partial workload suspension is not supported.
+
+## Proposal
+
+<!--
+This is where we get down to the specifics of what the proposal actually is.
+This should have enough detail that reviewers can understand exactly what
+you're proposing, but should not include things like API designs or
+implementation. What is the desired outcome and how do we measure success?.
+The "Design Details" section below is for the real
+nitty-gritty.
+-->
+This enhancement proposes the introduction of the following APIs to influence
+Workload preemption:
+- A field in the Workload to describe the cost of preempting a Workload at a
+  given time.
+- A field in the LocalQueue to set Workload defaults.
+- A field in the ClusterQueue to influence how long to wait before starting
+  preemption.
+
+And an algorithm for selecting a set of Workloads using borrowed quota to be
+preempted when a Workload in a ClusterQueue is under the min quota.
+
+### User Stories (Optional)
+
+<!--
+Detail the things that people will be able to do if this KEP is implemented.
+Include as much detail as possible so that people can understand the "how" of
+the system. The goal here is to make this feel real for users without getting
+bogged down.
+-->
+
+#### Story 1
+
+As a batch user, I know how safe it is to disrupt some workloads after they
+have been running for some time.
+
+An sample Workload has the following characteristics:
+Early in their execution, the Workload hasn't made much progress, so it can be
+preempted. Later, the Worload is on the path of doing significant progress, so
+it's best not to disturb it. Lastly, the Workload is expected to have made
+some checkpoints, so it's ok to disturb it.
+
+This can be expressed with the following configuration:
+
+```yaml
+apiVersion: kueue.x-k8s.io/v1alpha2
+kind: Workload
+metadata:
+  name: my-workload
+spec:
+  preemption:
+    disruptionCostMilestones:
+    - seconds: 60
+      cost: 100
+    - seconds: 600
+      cost: 0
+```
+
+The cost is a linear interpolation of the configuration above. A graphical
+representation of the cost looks like the following (not to scale):
+
+```
+cost
+
+100      __
+        /  \___
+       /       \___
+      /            \___
+0    /                 \_
+    0    60             600  time
+```
+
+As a cluster administrator, I can configure default `disruptionCostMilestones`
+for certain workloads using webhooks or setting them for all Workloads in a
+LocalQueue:
+
+```yaml
+apiVersion: kueue.x-k8s.io/v1alpha2
+kind: LocalQueue
+metadata:
+  name: cluster-total
+spec:
+  preemptionDefaults:
+    disruptionCostMilestones:
+    - seconds: 60
+      cost: 100
+    - seconds: 600
+      cost: 0
+```
+
+#### Story 2
+
+As a cluster administrator, in the cluster I have cohorts with different
+characteristics:
+
+- In some cohorts, all the ClusterQueues are known to have short-running
+  workloads that don't do checkpointing. In this case, it's fine to wait for
+  jobs to finish, but don't allow ClusterQueues to borrow more resources.
+- In other cohorts, ClusterQueues might have long running jobs that support
+  checkpointing. In this case, it's necessary to preempt Workloads that are
+  using borrowed quota.
+
+I want to configure in the ClusterQueue how long to wait for Workloads to finish
+up to a limit.
+
+```yaml
+apiVersion: kueue.x-k8s.io/v1alpha2
+kind: ClusterQueue
+metadata:
+  name: cluster-total
+spec:
+  preemption:
+    waitBeforePreemptionSeconds: 600 # leave empty to wait indefinitely.
+```
+
+### Notes/Contraints/Caveats (Optional)
+
+<!--
+What are the caveats to the proposal?
+What are some important details that didn't come across above?
+Go in to as much detail as necessary here.
+This might be a good place to talk about core concepts and how they relate.
+-->
+
+### Risks and Mitigations
+
+<!--
+What are the risks of this proposal, and how do we mitigate? Think broadly.
+For example, consider both security and how this will impact the larger
+Kubernetes ecosystem.
+
+How will security be reviewed, and by whom?
+
+How will UX be reviewed, and by whom?
+
+Consider including folks who also work outside the SIG or subproject.
+-->
+### Increased admission latency
+
+## Design Details
+
+The proposal consists of new API fields and a preemption algorithm.
+
+### API
+
+#### Workload changes
+
+The new API fields in a Workload describe the policies and preferences for
+preempting the Workload.
+
+```golang
+
+const WorkloadStarted = "Started"
+
+type WorkloadSpec struct {
+  ...
+  // preemption describes the policies and preferences for preempting this
+  // Workload.
+  // preemption is immutable once set.
+  Preemption *WorkloadPreemption
+}
+
+type WorklodPreemption struct {
+  // disruptionCostMilestones defines a cost curve for preempting this workload
+  // based on the time the workload has been running.
+  // The curve is defined by points of (time, cost). The cost is calculated from
+  // a linear interpolation of the cost between the two time points that contain
+  // the current workload runtime.
+  // The first point is implicitly (0, 0)
+  // The cost from the last milestone is constant.
+  //
+  // For example: given the points (60, 0) and (120, 100), the cost of
+  // preempting the Workload after it has been running for:
+  // - 30s is 0,
+  // - 90s is 50,
+  // - 300s is 100.
+  //
+  // disruptionCostMilestones[i].time > disruptioncostMilestones[j].time for i > j
+  DisruptionCostMilestones []DisruptionCostMilestone.
+}
+
+type DisruptionCostMilestone struct {
+  // seconds is the time since the Workload started (timestamp of the Started
+  // condition on the Workload).
+  Seconds int64
+
+  // cost is an integer representation of the cost of disrupting the Workload
+  // at a given time. The value has meaning when compared to the cost of
+  // disrupting other Workloads.
+  //
+  // 0 <= cost <= 100
+  Cost int32
+}
+```
+
+#### LocalQueue changes
+
+The new API fields in LocalQueue describe the defaults that apply to Workloads
+in this LocalQueue.
+
+```golang
+type LocalQueueSpec struct {
+  ...
+  // preemptionDefaults are the defaults applied to Workloads assigned to this
+  // LocalQueue. The defauts are applied to Workloads at the time of the
+  // Workload admission.
+  PreemptionDefaults *WorkloadPreemption
+}
+```
+
+#### ClusterQueue changes
+
+The new API fields in ClusterQueue describe how to influence the selection
+of Workloads to preempt.
+
+```golang
+const ClusterQueuePendingWorkloadsUnderMinQuota = "PendingWorkloadsUnderMinQuota"
```

  Added

- **keps/83-workload-preemption/README.md:221** by [@alculquicondor](https://github.com/alculquicondor) — 2022-10-07T13:55:14Z

+## Summary
+

+workloads in ClusterQueues that are borrowing resources from the cohort
+when other ClusterQueues under their minimum quota need the resources.
+
+API fields in the Workload, LocalQueue and ClusterQueue objects control
+different preemption policies.
+
+## Motivation

+when ClusterQueues under their minimum quota lend resources, they should
+be able to recover those resources fast, to be able to admit workloads
+when there are sudden spikes.
+
+Currently, the only mechanism to recover those resources is to wait for
+Workloads to finish, which is generally unbounded.
+

+
+- Preempt Workloads from ClusterQueues borrowing resources when other
+  ClusterQueues in the cohort, under their minimum quota, need the resources.
+- Introduce API fields in Workload, LocalQueue and ClusterQueue to inform
+  which Workloads can be preempted and in which order.
+
+### Non-Goals
+
+- Graceful termination of Workloads is left to the controller that integrates
+  with the particular workload API.
+  For example, the integration with Job uses the [suspend field](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/job/#suspending-a-job).
+- Partial workload suspension is not supported.
+
+## Proposal
+
+<!--
+This is where we get down to the specifics of what the proposal actually is.
+This should have enough detail that reviewers can understand exactly what
+you're proposing, but should not include things like API designs or
+implementation. What is the desired outcome and how do we measure success?.
+The "Design Details" section below is for the real
+nitty-gritty.
+-->
+This enhancement proposes the introduction of the following APIs to influence
+Workload preemption:
+- A field in the Workload to describe the cost of preempting a Workload at a
+  given time.
+- A field in the LocalQueue to set Workload defaults.
+- A field in the ClusterQueue to influence how long to wait before starting
+  preemption.
+
+And an algorithm for selecting a set of Workloads using borrowed quota to be
+preempted when a Workload in a ClusterQueue is under the min quota.
+
+### User Stories (Optional)
+
+<!--
+Detail the things that people will be able to do if this KEP is implemented.
+Include as much detail as possible so that people can understand the "how" of
+the system. The goal here is to make this feel real for users without getting
+bogged down.
+-->
+
+#### Story 1
+
+As a batch user, I know how safe it is to disrupt some workloads after they
+have been running for some time.
+
+An sample Workload has the following characteristics:
+Early in their execution, the Workload hasn't made much progress, so it can be
+preempted. Later, the Worload is on the path of doing significant progress, so
+it's best not to disturb it. Lastly, the Workload is expected to have made
+some checkpoints, so it's ok to disturb it.
+
+This can be expressed with the following configuration:
+
+```yaml
+apiVersion: kueue.x-k8s.io/v1alpha2

+  name: my-workload
+spec:
+  preemption:
+    disruptionCostMilestones:
+    - seconds: 60
+      cost: 100
+    - seconds: 600
+      cost: 0
+```
+
+The cost is a linear interpolation of the configuration above. A graphical
+representation of the cost looks like the following (not to scale):
+
+```
+cost
+
+100      __
+        /  \___
+       /       \___
+      /            \___
+0    /                 \_
+    0    60             600  time
+```
+
+As a cluster administrator, I can configure default `disruptionCostMilestones`
+for certain workloads using webhooks or setting them for all Workloads in a
+LocalQueue:
+
+```yaml
+apiVersion: kueue.x-k8s.io/v1alpha2
+kind: LocalQueue
+metadata:
+  name: cluster-total
+spec:
+  preemptionDefaults:
+    disruptionCostMilestones:
+    - seconds: 60
+      cost: 100
+    - seconds: 600
+      cost: 0
+```
+
+#### Story 2
+
+As a cluster administrator, in the cluster I have cohorts with different
+characteristics:
+
+- In some cohorts, all the ClusterQueues are known to have short-running
+  workloads that don't do checkpointing. In this case, it's fine to wait for
+  jobs to finish, but don't allow ClusterQueues to borrow more resources.
+- In other cohorts, ClusterQueues might have long running jobs that support
+  checkpointing. In this case, it's necessary to preempt Workloads that are
+  using borrowed quota.
+
+I want to configure in the ClusterQueue how long to wait for Workloads to finish
+up to a limit.
+
+```yaml
+apiVersion: kueue.x-k8s.io/v1alpha2
+kind: ClusterQueue
+metadata:
+  name: cluster-total
+spec:
+  preemption:
+    waitBeforePreemptionSeconds: 600 # leave empty to wait indefinitely.
+```
+
+### Notes/Contraints/Caveats (Optional)
+
+<!--
+What are the caveats to the proposal?
+What are some important details that didn't come across above?
+Go in to as much detail as necessary here.
+This might be a good place to talk about core concepts and how they relate.
+-->
+
+### Risks and Mitigations
+
+<!--
+What are the risks of this proposal, and how do we mitigate? Think broadly.
+For example, consider both security and how this will impact the larger
+Kubernetes ecosystem.

+### Increased admission latency
+
+## Design Details
+
+The proposal consists of new API fields and a preemption algorithm.
+
+### API
+
+#### Workload changes
+
+The new API fields in a Workload describe the policies and preferences for
+preempting the Workload.
+
+```golang
+
+const WorkloadStarted = "Started"

+type WorkloadSpec struct {
+  ...
+  // preemption describes the policies and preferences for preempting this
+  // Workload.
+  // preemption is immutable once set.
+  Preemption *WorkloadPreemption
+}
+
+type WorklodPreemption struct {
+  // disruptionCostMilestones defines a cost curve for preempting this workload
+  // based on the time the workload has been running.
+  // The curve is defined by points of (time, cost). The cost is calculated from
+  // a linear interpolation of the cost between the two time points that contain
+  // the current workload runtime.
+  // The first point is implicitly (0, 0)
+  // The cost from the last milestone is constant.
+  //
+  // For example: given the points (60, 0) and (120, 100), the cost of
+  // preempting the Workload after it has been running for:
+  // - 30s is 0,
+  // - 90s is 50,
+  // - 300s is 100.
+  //
+  // disruptionCostMilestones[i].time > disruptioncostMilestones[j].time for i > j
+  DisruptionCostMilestones []DisruptionCostMilestone.
+}
+
+type DisruptionCostMilestone struct {
+  // seconds is the time since the Workload started (timestamp of the Started
+  // condition on the Workload).
+  Seconds int64
+
+  // cost is an integer representation of the cost of disrupting the Workload
+  // at a given time. The value has meaning when compared to the cost of
+  // disrupting other Workloads.
+  //
+  // 0 <= cost <= 100
+  Cost int32
+}
+```
+
+#### LocalQueue changes
+
+The new API fields in LocalQueue describe the defaults that apply to Workloads
+in this LocalQueue.
+
+```golang
+type LocalQueueSpec struct {
+  ...
+  // preemptionDefaults are the defaults applied to Workloads assigned to this
+  // LocalQueue. The defauts are applied to Workloads at the time of the
+  // Workload admission.
+  PreemptionDefaults *WorkloadPreemption
+}
+```
+
+#### ClusterQueue changes
+
+The new API fields in ClusterQueue describe how to influence the selection
+of Workloads to preempt.
+
+```golang
+const ClusterQueuePendingWorkloadsUnderMinQuota = "PendingWorkloadsUnderMinQuota"
+
+type ClusterQueueSpec struct {
+  ...
+  // preemption describes policies to preempt Workloads from ClusterQueues
+  // borrowing resources when this ClusterQueue has pending Workloads and the
+  // min quota is unmet.
+  preemption ClusterQueuePreemption
+}
+
+type ClusterQueueStatus struct {
+  ...
+  conditions []metav1.Condition
+}
+
+type ClusterQueuePreemption struct {
```

  If so, I think it's explained below how they work together.

+## Summary

+workloads in ClusterQueues that are borrowing resources from the cohort
+when other ClusterQueues under their minimum quota need the resources.

+API fields in the Workload, LocalQueue and ClusterQueue objects control
+different preemption policies.
+
+## Motivation

+when ClusterQueues under their minimum quota lend resources, they should
+be able to recover those resources fast, to be able to admit workloads
+when there are sudden spikes.
+
+Currently, the only mechanism to recover those resources is to wait for
+Workloads to finish, which is generally unbounded.
+
+### Goals
+
+- Preempt Workloads from ClusterQueues borrowing resources when other
+  ClusterQueues in the cohort, under their minimum quota, need the resources.
+- Introduce API fields in Workload, LocalQueue and ClusterQueue to inform
+  which Workloads can be preempted and in which order.
+
+### Non-Goals
