+- Graceful termination of Workloads is left to the controller that integrates
+  with the particular workload API.
+  For example, the integration with Job uses the [suspend field](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/job/#suspending-a-job).
+- Partial workload suspension is not supported.
+
+## Proposal
+
+<!--
+This is where we get down to the specifics of what the proposal actually is.
+This should have enough detail that reviewers can understand exactly what
+you're proposing, but should not include things like API designs or
+implementation. What is the desired outcome and how do we measure success?.
+The "Design Details" section below is for the real
+nitty-gritty.
+-->
+This enhancement proposes the introduction of the following APIs to influence
+Workload preemption:
+- A field in the Workload to describe the cost of preempting a Workload at a
+  given time.
+- A field in the LocalQueue to set Workload defaults.
+- A field in the ClusterQueue to influence how long to wait before starting
+  preemption.
+
+And an algorithm for selecting a set of Workloads using borrowed quota to be
+preempted when a Workload in a ClusterQueue is under the min quota.
+
+### User Stories (Optional)
+
+<!--
+Detail the things that people will be able to do if this KEP is implemented.
+Include as much detail as possible so that people can understand the "how" of
+the system. The goal here is to make this feel real for users without getting
+bogged down.
+-->
+
+#### Story 1
+
+As a batch user, I know how safe it is to disrupt some workloads after they
+have been running for some time.
+
+An sample Workload has the following characteristics:
+Early in their execution, the Workload hasn't made much progress, so it can be
+preempted. Later, the Worload is on the path of doing significant progress, so
+it's best not to disturb it. Lastly, the Workload is expected to have made
+some checkpoints, so it's ok to disturb it.
+
+This can be expressed with the following configuration:
+
+```yaml
+apiVersion: kueue.x-k8s.io/v1alpha2
+kind: Workload
+metadata:
+  name: my-workload
+spec:
+  preemption:
+    disruptionCostMilestones:
+    - seconds: 60
+      cost: 100
+    - seconds: 600
+      cost: 0
+```
+
+The cost is a linear interpolation of the configuration above. A graphical
+representation of the cost looks like the following (not to scale):
+
+```
+cost
+
+100      __
+        /  \___
+       /       \___
+      /            \___
+0    /                 \_
+    0    60             600  time
+```
+
+As a cluster administrator, I can configure default `disruptionCostMilestones`
+for certain workloads using webhooks or setting them for all Workloads in a
+LocalQueue:
+
+```yaml
+apiVersion: kueue.x-k8s.io/v1alpha2
+kind: LocalQueue
+metadata:
+  name: cluster-total
+spec:
+  preemptionDefaults:
+    disruptionCostMilestones:
+    - seconds: 60
+      cost: 100
+    - seconds: 600
+      cost: 0
+```
+
+#### Story 2
+
+As a cluster administrator, in the cluster I have cohorts with different
+characteristics:
+
+- In some cohorts, all the ClusterQueues are known to have short-running
+  workloads that don't do checkpointing. In this case, it's fine to wait for
+  jobs to finish, but don't allow ClusterQueues to borrow more resources.
+- In other cohorts, ClusterQueues might have long running jobs that support
+  checkpointing. In this case, it's necessary to preempt Workloads that are
+  using borrowed quota.
+
+I want to configure in the ClusterQueue how long to wait for Workloads to finish
+up to a limit.
+
+```yaml
+apiVersion: kueue.x-k8s.io/v1alpha2
+kind: ClusterQueue
+metadata:
+  name: cluster-total
+spec:
+  preemption:
+    waitBeforePreemptionSeconds: 600 # leave empty to wait indefinitely.
+```
+
+### Notes/Contraints/Caveats (Optional)
+
+<!--
+What are the caveats to the proposal?
+What are some important details that didn't come across above?
+Go in to as much detail as necessary here.
+This might be a good place to talk about core concepts and how they relate.
+-->
+
+### Risks and Mitigations
+
+<!--
+What are the risks of this proposal, and how do we mitigate? Think broadly.
+For example, consider both security and how this will impact the larger
+Kubernetes ecosystem.
+
+How will security be reviewed, and by whom?
+
+How will UX be reviewed, and by whom?
+
+Consider including folks who also work outside the SIG or subproject.
+-->
+### Increased admission latency
+
+## Design Details
+
+The proposal consists of new API fields and a preemption algorithm.
+
+### API
+
+#### Workload changes
+
+The new API fields in a Workload describe the policies and preferences for
+preempting the Workload.
+
+```golang
+
+const WorkloadStarted = "Started"
+
+type WorkloadSpec struct {
+  ...
+  // preemption describes the policies and preferences for preempting this
+  // Workload.
+  // preemption is immutable once set.
+  Preemption *WorkloadPreemption
+}
+
+type WorklodPreemption struct {
+  // disruptionCostMilestones defines a cost curve for preempting this workload
+  // based on the time the workload has been running.
+  // The curve is defined by points of (time, cost). The cost is calculated from
+  // a linear interpolation of the cost between the two time points that contain
+  // the current workload runtime.
+  // The first point is implicitly (0, 0)
+  // The cost from the last milestone is constant.
+  //
+  // For example: given the points (60, 0) and (120, 100), the cost of
+  // preempting the Workload after it has been running for:
+  // - 30s is 0,
+  // - 90s is 50,
+  // - 300s is 100.
+  //
+  // disruptionCostMilestones[i].time > disruptioncostMilestones[j].time for i > j
+  DisruptionCostMilestones []DisruptionCostMilestone.
+}
+
+type DisruptionCostMilestone struct {
+  // seconds is the time since the Workload started (timestamp of the Started
+  // condition on the Workload).
+  Seconds int64
+
+  // cost is an integer representation of the cost of disrupting the Workload
+  // at a given time. The value has meaning when compared to the cost of
+  // disrupting other Workloads.
+  //
+  // 0 <= cost <= 100
+  Cost int32
+}
+```
+
+#### LocalQueue changes
+
+The new API fields in LocalQueue describe the defaults that apply to Workloads
+in this LocalQueue.
+
+```golang
+type LocalQueueSpec struct {
+  ...
+  // preemptionDefaults are the defaults applied to Workloads assigned to this
+  // LocalQueue. The defauts are applied to Workloads at the time of the
+  // Workload admission.
+  PreemptionDefaults *WorkloadPreemption
+}
+```
+
+#### ClusterQueue changes
+
+The new API fields in ClusterQueue describe how to influence the selection
+of Workloads to preempt.
+
+```golang
+const ClusterQueuePendingWorkloadsUnderMinQuota = "PendingWorkloadsUnderMinQuota"
+
+type ClusterQueueSpec struct {
+  ...
+  // preemption describes policies to preempt Workloads from ClusterQueues
+  // borrowing resources when this ClusterQueue has pending Workloads and the
+  // min quota is unmet.
+  preemption ClusterQueuePreemption
+}
+
+type ClusterQueueStatus struct {
+  ...
+  conditions []metav1.Condition
+}
+
+type ClusterQueuePreemption struct {
+  // waitBeforePreemptionSeconds is the time in seconds that the ClusterQueue
+  // is willing to wait for Workloads to finish before issuing preemptions,
+  // since the first time that the ClusterQueue has pending workloads that would
+  // fit under the min quota. This time is present in the timestamp for the
+  // PendingWorkloadsUnderMinQuota condition.
+  // If null, it means to wait indefinitely.
+  WaitBeforePreemptionSeconds *int64
+
+  // <UNRESOLVED>
+
+  // allowPreemptionByLowerPriority determines whether pending Workloads can
+  // preempt Workloads with higher priority in this ClusterQueue.
```

  higher is right. Maybe I need to adjust the field name?

- **keps/83-workload-preemption/README.md:327** by [@alculquicondor](https://github.com/alculquicondor) — 2022-10-07T13:58:47Z
```diff
@@ -0,0 +1,449 @@
+# KEP-83: Workload preemption
+
+<!-- toc -->
+- [Summary](#summary)
+- [Motivation](#motivation)
+  - [Goals](#goals)
+  - [Non-Goals](#non-goals)
+- [Proposal](#proposal)
+  - [User Stories (Optional)](#user-stories-optional)
+    - [Story 1](#story-1)
+    - [Story 2](#story-2)
+  - [Notes/Contraints/Caveats (Optional)](#notescontraintscaveats-optional)
+  - [Risks and Mitigations](#risks-and-mitigations)
+- [Design Details](#design-details)
+  - [Test Plan](#test-plan)
+      - [Prerequisite testing updates](#prerequisite-testing-updates)
+    - [Unit Tests](#unit-tests)
+    - [Integration tests](#integration-tests)
+  - [Graduation Criteria](#graduation-criteria)
+- [Implementation History](#implementation-history)
+- [Drawbacks](#drawbacks)
+- [Alternatives](#alternatives)
+<!-- /toc -->
+
+## Summary
+
+This enhancement introduces workload preemption, a mechanism to terminate
+workloads in ClusterQueues that are borrowing resources from the cohort
+when other ClusterQueues under their minimum quota need the resources.
+
+API fields in the Workload, LocalQueue and ClusterQueue objects control
+different preemption policies.
+
+## Motivation
+
+when ClusterQueues under their minimum quota lend resources, they should
+be able to recover those resources fast, to be able to admit workloads
+when there are sudden spikes.
+
+Currently, the only mechanism to recover those resources is to wait for
+Workloads to finish, which is generally unbounded.
+
+### Goals
+
+- Preempt Workloads from ClusterQueues borrowing resources when other
+  ClusterQueues in the cohort, under their minimum quota, need the resources.
+- Introduce API fields in Workload, LocalQueue and ClusterQueue to inform
+  which Workloads can be preempted and in which order.
+
+### Non-Goals
+
+- Graceful termination of Workloads is left to the controller that integrates
+  with the particular workload API.
+  For example, the integration with Job uses the [suspend field](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/job/#suspending-a-job).
+- Partial workload suspension is not supported.
+
+## Proposal
+
+<!--
+This is where we get down to the specifics of what the proposal actually is.
+This should have enough detail that reviewers can understand exactly what
+you're proposing, but should not include things like API designs or
+implementation. What is the desired outcome and how do we measure success?.
+The "Design Details" section below is for the real
+nitty-gritty.
+-->
+This enhancement proposes the introduction of the following APIs to influence
+Workload preemption:
+- A field in the Workload to describe the cost of preempting a Workload at a
+  given time.
+- A field in the LocalQueue to set Workload defaults.
+- A field in the ClusterQueue to influence how long to wait before starting
+  preemption.
+
+And an algorithm for selecting a set of Workloads using borrowed quota to be
+preempted when a Workload in a ClusterQueue is under the min quota.
+
+### User Stories (Optional)
+
+<!--
+Detail the things that people will be able to do if this KEP is implemented.
+Include as much detail as possible so that people can understand the "how" of
+the system. The goal here is to make this feel real for users without getting
+bogged down.
+-->
+
+#### Story 1
+
+As a batch user, I know how safe it is to disrupt some workloads after they
+have been running for some time.
+
+An sample Workload has the following characteristics:
+Early in their execution, the Workload hasn't made much progress, so it can be
+preempted. Later, the Worload is on the path of doing significant progress, so
+it's best not to disturb it. Lastly, the Workload is expected to have made
+some checkpoints, so it's ok to disturb it.
+
+This can be expressed with the following configuration:
+
+```yaml
+apiVersion: kueue.x-k8s.io/v1alpha2
+kind: Workload
+metadata:
+  name: my-workload
+spec:
+  preemption:
+    disruptionCostMilestones:
+    - seconds: 60
+      cost: 100
+    - seconds: 600
+      cost: 0
+```
+
+The cost is a linear interpolation of the configuration above. A graphical
+representation of the cost looks like the following (not to scale):
+
+```
+cost
+
+100      __
+        /  \___
+       /       \___
+      /            \___
+0    /                 \_
+    0    60             600  time
+```
+
+As a cluster administrator, I can configure default `disruptionCostMilestones`
+for certain workloads using webhooks or setting them for all Workloads in a
+LocalQueue:
+
+```yaml
+apiVersion: kueue.x-k8s.io/v1alpha2
+kind: LocalQueue
+metadata:
+  name: cluster-total
+spec:
+  preemptionDefaults:
+    disruptionCostMilestones:
+    - seconds: 60
+      cost: 100
+    - seconds: 600
+      cost: 0
+```
+
+#### Story 2
+
+As a cluster administrator, in the cluster I have cohorts with different
+characteristics:
+
+- In some cohorts, all the ClusterQueues are known to have short-running
+  workloads that don't do checkpointing. In this case, it's fine to wait for
+  jobs to finish, but don't allow ClusterQueues to borrow more resources.
+- In other cohorts, ClusterQueues might have long running jobs that support
+  checkpointing. In this case, it's necessary to preempt Workloads that are
+  using borrowed quota.
+
+I want to configure in the ClusterQueue how long to wait for Workloads to finish
+up to a limit.
+
+```yaml
+apiVersion: kueue.x-k8s.io/v1alpha2
+kind: ClusterQueue
+metadata:
+  name: cluster-total
+spec:
+  preemption:
+    waitBeforePreemptionSeconds: 600 # leave empty to wait indefinitely.
+```
+
+### Notes/Contraints/Caveats (Optional)
+
+<!--
+What are the caveats to the proposal?
+What are some important details that didn't come across above?
+Go in to as much detail as necessary here.
+This might be a good place to talk about core concepts and how they relate.
+-->
+
+### Risks and Mitigations
+
+<!--
+What are the risks of this proposal, and how do we mitigate? Think broadly.
+For example, consider both security and how this will impact the larger
+Kubernetes ecosystem.
+
+How will security be reviewed, and by whom?
+
+How will UX be reviewed, and by whom?
+
+Consider including folks who also work outside the SIG or subproject.
+-->
+### Increased admission latency
+
+## Design Details
+
+The proposal consists of new API fields and a preemption algorithm.
+
+### API
+
+#### Workload changes
+
+The new API fields in a Workload describe the policies and preferences for
+preempting the Workload.
+
+```golang
+
+const WorkloadStarted = "Started"
+
+type WorkloadSpec struct {
+  ...
+  // preemption describes the policies and preferences for preempting this
+  // Workload.
+  // preemption is immutable once set.
+  Preemption *WorkloadPreemption
+}
+
+type WorklodPreemption struct {
+  // disruptionCostMilestones defines a cost curve for preempting this workload
+  // based on the time the workload has been running.
+  // The curve is defined by points of (time, cost). The cost is calculated from
+  // a linear interpolation of the cost between the two time points that contain
+  // the current workload runtime.
+  // The first point is implicitly (0, 0)
+  // The cost from the last milestone is constant.
+  //
+  // For example: given the points (60, 0) and (120, 100), the cost of
+  // preempting the Workload after it has been running for:
+  // - 30s is 0,
+  // - 90s is 50,
+  // - 300s is 100.
+  //
+  // disruptionCostMilestones[i].time > disruptioncostMilestones[j].time for i > j
+  DisruptionCostMilestones []DisruptionCostMilestone.
+}
+
+type DisruptionCostMilestone struct {
+  // seconds is the time since the Workload started (timestamp of the Started
+  // condition on the Workload).
+  Seconds int64
+
+  // cost is an integer representation of the cost of disrupting the Workload
+  // at a given time. The value has meaning when compared to the cost of
+  // disrupting other Workloads.
+  //
+  // 0 <= cost <= 100
+  Cost int32
+}
+```
+
+#### LocalQueue changes
+
+The new API fields in LocalQueue describe the defaults that apply to Workloads
+in this LocalQueue.
+
+```golang
+type LocalQueueSpec struct {
+  ...
+  // preemptionDefaults are the defaults applied to Workloads assigned to this
+  // LocalQueue. The defauts are applied to Workloads at the time of the
+  // Workload admission.
+  PreemptionDefaults *WorkloadPreemption
+}
+```
+
+#### ClusterQueue changes
+
+The new API fields in ClusterQueue describe how to influence the selection
+of Workloads to preempt.
+
+```golang
+const ClusterQueuePendingWorkloadsUnderMinQuota = "PendingWorkloadsUnderMinQuota"
+
+type ClusterQueueSpec struct {
+  ...
+  // preemption describes policies to preempt Workloads from ClusterQueues
+  // borrowing resources when this ClusterQueue has pending Workloads and the
+  // min quota is unmet.
+  preemption ClusterQueuePreemption
+}
+
+type ClusterQueueStatus struct {
+  ...
+  conditions []metav1.Condition
+}
+
+type ClusterQueuePreemption struct {
+  // waitBeforePreemptionSeconds is the time in seconds that the ClusterQueue
+  // is willing to wait for Workloads to finish before issuing preemptions,
+  // since the first time that the ClusterQueue has pending workloads that would
+  // fit under the min quota. This time is present in the timestamp for the
+  // PendingWorkloadsUnderMinQuota condition.
+  // If null, it means to wait indefinitely.
+  WaitBeforePreemptionSeconds *int64
+
+  // <UNRESOLVED>
+
+  // allowPreemptionByLowerPriority determines whether pending Workloads can
+  // preempt Workloads with higher priority in this ClusterQueue.
+  // Defaults to false.
+  AllowPreemptionByLowerPriority bool
+
+  // workloadSorting determines how Workloads from the cohort that are
+  // candidates for preemption are sorted.
+  // Sorting happens at the time when a Workload in this ClusterQueue is
+  // evaluated for admission. All the Workloads in the cohort are sorted based
+  // on the criteria defined in the preempting ClusterQueue.
+  // workloadOrder is a list of comparison criteria between two Workloads that
+  // are evaluated in order.
+  // Possible criteria are:
+  // - ByLowestPriority: Prefer to preempt the Workload with lower priority.
+  // - ByLowestCost: Prefer to preempt the workload with lower preemption cost.
+  // - ByLowestRuntime: Prefer to preempt the Workload that started more
+  //   recently.
+  // - ByLongestRuntime: Prefer to preempt the Workload that started earlier.
+  //
+  // If empty, the behavior is equivalent to
+  // [ByLowestPriority, ByLowestCost, ByLowestRuntime].
+  WorkloadSorting []WorkloadComparison
+
+  type WorkloadSortingCriteria string
+
+  const (
+    ComparisonByLowestPriority = "ByLowestPriority"
+    ComparisonByLowestCost = "ByLowestCost"
+    ComparisonByLowestRuntime = "ByLowestRuntime"
+  )
```

  That's my thinking too. I'll leave this for a bit as a documentation of the default behavior, while we get some feedback on the API proposal. If we decide not to include this API, I'll move it to the design details.

### Review by [@ahg-g](https://github.com/ahg-g) (commented) — 2022-10-11T14:25:50Z

+## Summary

+- Partial workload suspension is not supported.

+## Proposal

+  preemption.

+preempted when a Workload in a ClusterQueue is under the min quota.
+
+### User Stories (Optional)
+
+<!--

+Include as much detail as possible so that people can understand the "how" of

+bogged down.
+-->
+
+#### Story 1
+
+As a batch user, I know how safe it is to disrupt some workloads after they

+
+An sample Workload has the following characteristics:
+Early in their execution, the Workload hasn't made much progress, so it can be
+preempted. Later, the Worload is on the path of doing significant progress, so
+it's best not to disturb it. Lastly, the Workload is expected to have made
+some checkpoints, so it's ok to disturb it.
+
+This can be expressed with the following configuration:
+
+```yaml

+  name: my-workload
+spec:

+    - seconds: 60

+```

+
+```
+cost
+
+100      __

+       /       \___
+      /            \___
+0    /                 \_
+    0    60             600  time
+```
+
+As a cluster administrator, I can configure default `disruptionCostMilestones`

+
+```yaml

+  name: cluster-total

+  preemptionDefaults:
+    disruptionCostMilestones:
+    - seconds: 60
+      cost: 100
+    - seconds: 600
+      cost: 0
+```
+
+#### Story 2
+
+As a cluster administrator, in the cluster I have cohorts with different
+characteristics:
+
+- In some cohorts, all the ClusterQueues are known to have short-running
+  workloads that don't do checkpointing. In this case, it's fine to wait for
+  jobs to finish, but don't allow ClusterQueues to borrow more resources.
+- In other cohorts, ClusterQueues might have long running jobs that support
+  checkpointing. In this case, it's necessary to preempt Workloads that are
+  using borrowed quota.
+
+I want to configure in the ClusterQueue how long to wait for Workloads to finish
+up to a limit.
+
+```yaml
+apiVersion: kueue.x-k8s.io/v1alpha2
+kind: ClusterQueue
+metadata:
+  name: cluster-total
+spec:
+  preemption:
+    waitBeforePreemptionSeconds: 600 # leave empty to wait indefinitely.
+```
+
+### Notes/Contraints/Caveats (Optional)
+
+<!--
+What are the caveats to the proposal?
+What are some important details that didn't come across above?
+Go in to as much detail as necessary here.
+This might be a good place to talk about core concepts and how they relate.
+-->
+
+### Risks and Mitigations
+
+<!--
+What are the risks of this proposal, and how do we mitigate? Think broadly.
+For example, consider both security and how this will impact the larger
+Kubernetes ecosystem.
+

+

+
+Consider including folks who also work outside the SIG or subproject.
+-->
+### Increased admission latency
+
+## Design Details
+
+The proposal consists of new API fields and a preemption algorithm.
+
+### API
+
+#### Workload changes
+
+The new API fields in a Workload describe the policies and preferences for
+preempting the Workload.
+
+```golang
+
+const WorkloadStarted = "Started"
+
+type WorkloadSpec struct {
+  ...
+  // preemption describes the policies and preferences for preempting this
+  // Workload.
+  // preemption is immutable once set.
+  Preemption *WorkloadPreemption
+}
+
+type WorklodPreemption struct {
+  // disruptionCostMilestones defines a cost curve for preempting this workload
+  // based on the time the workload has been running.
+  // The curve is defined by points of (time, cost). The cost is calculated from
+  // a linear interpolation of the cost between the two time points that contain
+  // the current workload runtime.
+  // The first point is implicitly (0, 0)
+  // The cost from the last milestone is constant.
+  //
+  // For example: given the points (60, 0) and (120, 100), the cost of
+  // preempting the Workload after it has been running for:
+  // - 30s is 0,
+  // - 90s is 50,
+  // - 300s is 100.
+  //
+  // disruptionCostMilestones[i].time > disruptioncostMilestones[j].time for i > j
+  DisruptionCostMilestones []DisruptionCostMilestone.
+}
+
+type DisruptionCostMilestone struct {
+  // seconds is the time since the Workload started (timestamp of the Started
+  // condition on the Workload).
+  Seconds int64
+
+  // cost is an integer representation of the cost of disrupting the Workload
+  // at a given time. The value has meaning when compared to the cost of
+  // disrupting other Workloads.
+  //
+  // 0 <= cost <= 100
+  Cost int32
+}
+```
+
+#### LocalQueue changes
+
+The new API fields in LocalQueue describe the defaults that apply to Workloads
+in this LocalQueue.
+
+```golang
+type LocalQueueSpec struct {
+  ...
+  // preemptionDefaults are the defaults applied to Workloads assigned to this
+  // LocalQueue. The defauts are applied to Workloads at the time of the
+  // Workload admission.
+  PreemptionDefaults *WorkloadPreemption
+}
+```
+
+#### ClusterQueue changes
+
+The new API fields in ClusterQueue describe how to influence the selection
+of Workloads to preempt.
+
+```golang
+const ClusterQueuePendingWorkloadsUnderMinQuota = "PendingWorkloadsUnderMinQuota"
+
+type ClusterQueueSpec struct {
+  ...
+  // preemption describes policies to preempt Workloads from ClusterQueues
+  // borrowing resources when this ClusterQueue has pending Workloads and the
+  // min quota is unmet.
+  preemption ClusterQueuePreemption
+}
+
+type ClusterQueueStatus struct {
+  ...
+  conditions []metav1.Condition
+}
+
+type ClusterQueuePreemption struct {
+  // waitBeforePreemptionSeconds is the time in seconds that the ClusterQueue
+  // is willing to wait for Workloads to finish before issuing preemptions,
+  // since the first time that the ClusterQueue has pending workloads that would
+  // fit under the min quota. This time is present in the timestamp for the
+  // PendingWorkloadsUnderMinQuota condition.
+  // If null, it means to wait indefinitely.
+  WaitBeforePreemptionSeconds *int64
+
+  // <UNRESOLVED>
+
+  // allowPreemptionByLowerPriority determines whether pending Workloads can
+  // preempt Workloads with higher priority in this ClusterQueue.
```

  I am missing something then, why would we want to allow lower priority workloads to preempt higher priority ones? or is this referring to higher priority workloads from other clusterQueues running on borrowed resources?

### Review by [@alculquicondor](https://github.com/alculquicondor) (commented) — 2022-10-11T15:31:05Z

- **keps/83-workload-preemption/README.md:299** by [@alculquicondor](https://github.com/alculquicondor) — 2022-10-11T15:31:05Z
```diff
@@ -0,0 +1,449 @@
+# KEP-83: Workload preemption
+
+<!-- toc -->
+- [Summary](#summary)
+- [Motivation](#motivation)
+  - [Goals](#goals)
+  - [Non-Goals](#non-goals)
+- [Proposal](#proposal)
+  - [User Stories (Optional)](#user-stories-optional)
+    - [Story 1](#story-1)
+    - [Story 2](#story-2)
+  - [Notes/Contraints/Caveats (Optional)](#notescontraintscaveats-optional)
+  - [Risks and Mitigations](#risks-and-mitigations)
+- [Design Details](#design-details)
+  - [Test Plan](#test-plan)
+      - [Prerequisite testing updates](#prerequisite-testing-updates)
+    - [Unit Tests](#unit-tests)
+    - [Integration tests](#integration-tests)
+  - [Graduation Criteria](#graduation-criteria)
+- [Implementation History](#implementation-history)
+- [Drawbacks](#drawbacks)
+- [Alternatives](#alternatives)
+<!-- /toc -->
+
+## Summary
+
+This enhancement introduces workload preemption, a mechanism to terminate
+workloads in ClusterQueues that are borrowing resources from the cohort
+when other ClusterQueues under their minimum quota need the resources.
+
+API fields in the Workload, LocalQueue and ClusterQueue objects control
+different preemption policies.
+
+## Motivation
+
+when ClusterQueues under their minimum quota lend resources, they should
+be able to recover those resources fast, to be able to admit workloads
+when there are sudden spikes.
+
+Currently, the only mechanism to recover those resources is to wait for
+Workloads to finish, which is generally unbounded.
+
+### Goals
+
+- Preempt Workloads from ClusterQueues borrowing resources when other
+  ClusterQueues in the cohort, under their minimum quota, need the resources.
+- Introduce API fields in Workload, LocalQueue and ClusterQueue to inform
+  which Workloads can be preempted and in which order.
+
+### Non-Goals
+
+- Graceful termination of Workloads is left to the controller that integrates
+  with the particular workload API.
+  For example, the integration with Job uses the [suspend field](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/job/#suspending-a-job).
+- Partial workload suspension is not supported.
+
+## Proposal
+
+<!--
+This is where we get down to the specifics of what the proposal actually is.
+This should have enough detail that reviewers can understand exactly what
+you're proposing, but should not include things like API designs or
+implementation. What is the desired outcome and how do we measure success?.
+The "Design Details" section below is for the real
+nitty-gritty.
+-->
+This enhancement proposes the introduction of the following APIs to influence
+Workload preemption:
+- A field in the Workload to describe the cost of preempting a Workload at a
+  given time.
+- A field in the LocalQueue to set Workload defaults.
+- A field in the ClusterQueue to influence how long to wait before starting
+  preemption.
+
+And an algorithm for selecting a set of Workloads using borrowed quota to be
+preempted when a Workload in a ClusterQueue is under the min quota.
+
+### User Stories (Optional)
+
+<!--
+Detail the things that people will be able to do if this KEP is implemented.
+Include as much detail as possible so that people can understand the "how" of
+the system. The goal here is to make this feel real for users without getting
+bogged down.
+-->
+
+#### Story 1
+
+As a batch user, I know how safe it is to disrupt some workloads after they
+have been running for some time.
+
+An sample Workload has the following characteristics:
+Early in their execution, the Workload hasn't made much progress, so it can be
+preempted. Later, the Worload is on the path of doing significant progress, so
+it's best not to disturb it. Lastly, the Workload is expected to have made
+some checkpoints, so it's ok to disturb it.
+
+This can be expressed with the following configuration:
+
+```yaml
+apiVersion: kueue.x-k8s.io/v1alpha2
+kind: Workload
+metadata:
+  name: my-workload
+spec:
+  preemption:
+    disruptionCostMilestones:
+    - seconds: 60
+      cost: 100
+    - seconds: 600
+      cost: 0
+```
+
+The cost is a linear interpolation of the configuration above. A graphical
+representation of the cost looks like the following (not to scale):
+
+```
+cost
+
+100      __
+        /  \___
+       /       \___
+      /            \___
+0    /                 \_
+    0    60             600  time
+```
+
+As a cluster administrator, I can configure default `disruptionCostMilestones`
+for certain workloads using webhooks or setting them for all Workloads in a
+LocalQueue:
+
+```yaml
+apiVersion: kueue.x-k8s.io/v1alpha2
+kind: LocalQueue
+metadata:
+  name: cluster-total
+spec:
+  preemptionDefaults:
+    disruptionCostMilestones:
+    - seconds: 60
+      cost: 100
+    - seconds: 600
+      cost: 0
+```
+
+#### Story 2
+
+As a cluster administrator, in the cluster I have cohorts with different
+characteristics:
+
+- In some cohorts, all the ClusterQueues are known to have short-running
+  workloads that don't do checkpointing. In this case, it's fine to wait for
+  jobs to finish, but don't allow ClusterQueues to borrow more resources.
+- In other cohorts, ClusterQueues might have long running jobs that support
+  checkpointing. In this case, it's necessary to preempt Workloads that are
+  using borrowed quota.
+
+I want to configure in the ClusterQueue how long to wait for Workloads to finish
+up to a limit.
+
+```yaml
+apiVersion: kueue.x-k8s.io/v1alpha2
+kind: ClusterQueue
+metadata:
+  name: cluster-total
+spec:
+  preemption:
+    waitBeforePreemptionSeconds: 600 # leave empty to wait indefinitely.
+```
+
+### Notes/Contraints/Caveats (Optional)
+
+<!--
+What are the caveats to the proposal?
+What are some important details that didn't come across above?
+Go in to as much detail as necessary here.
+This might be a good place to talk about core concepts and how they relate.
+-->
+
+### Risks and Mitigations
+
+<!--
+What are the risks of this proposal, and how do we mitigate? Think broadly.
+For example, consider both security and how this will impact the larger
+Kubernetes ecosystem.
+
+How will security be reviewed, and by whom?
+
+How will UX be reviewed, and by whom?
+
+Consider including folks who also work outside the SIG or subproject.
+-->
+### Increased admission latency
+
+## Design Details
+
+The proposal consists of new API fields and a preemption algorithm.
+
+### API
+
+#### Workload changes
+
+The new API fields in a Workload describe the policies and preferences for
+preempting the Workload.
+
+```golang
+
+const WorkloadStarted = "Started"
+
+type WorkloadSpec struct {
+  ...
+  // preemption describes the policies and preferences for preempting this
+  // Workload.
+  // preemption is immutable once set.
+  Preemption *WorkloadPreemption
+}
+
+type WorklodPreemption struct {
+  // disruptionCostMilestones defines a cost curve for preempting this workload
+  // based on the time the workload has been running.
+  // The curve is defined by points of (time, cost). The cost is calculated from
+  // a linear interpolation of the cost between the two time points that contain
+  // the current workload runtime.
+  // The first point is implicitly (0, 0)
+  // The cost from the last milestone is constant.
+  //
+  // For example: given the points (60, 0) and (120, 100), the cost of
+  // preempting the Workload after it has been running for:
+  // - 30s is 0,
+  // - 90s is 50,
+  // - 300s is 100.
+  //
+  // disruptionCostMilestones[i].time > disruptioncostMilestones[j].time for i > j
+  DisruptionCostMilestones []DisruptionCostMilestone.
+}
+
+type DisruptionCostMilestone struct {
+  // seconds is the time since the Workload started (timestamp of the Started
+  // condition on the Workload).
+  Seconds int64
+
+  // cost is an integer representation of the cost of disrupting the Workload
+  // at a given time. The value has meaning when compared to the cost of
+  // disrupting other Workloads.
+  //
+  // 0 <= cost <= 100
+  Cost int32
+}
+```
+
+#### LocalQueue changes
+
+The new API fields in LocalQueue describe the defaults that apply to Workloads
+in this LocalQueue.
+
+```golang
+type LocalQueueSpec struct {
+  ...
+  // preemptionDefaults are the defaults applied to Workloads assigned to this
+  // LocalQueue. The defauts are applied to Workloads at the time of the
+  // Workload admission.
+  PreemptionDefaults *WorkloadPreemption
+}
+```
+
+#### ClusterQueue changes
+
+The new API fields in ClusterQueue describe how to influence the selection
+of Workloads to preempt.
+
+```golang
+const ClusterQueuePendingWorkloadsUnderMinQuota = "PendingWorkloadsUnderMinQuota"
+
+type ClusterQueueSpec struct {
+  ...
+  // preemption describes policies to preempt Workloads from ClusterQueues
+  // borrowing resources when this ClusterQueue has pending Workloads and the
+  // min quota is unmet.
+  preemption ClusterQueuePreemption
+}
+
+type ClusterQueueStatus struct {
+  ...
+  conditions []metav1.Condition
+}
+
+type ClusterQueuePreemption struct {
+  // waitBeforePreemptionSeconds is the time in seconds that the ClusterQueue
+  // is willing to wait for Workloads to finish before issuing preemptions,
+  // since the first time that the ClusterQueue has pending workloads that would
+  // fit under the min quota. This time is present in the timestamp for the
+  // PendingWorkloadsUnderMinQuota condition.
+  // If null, it means to wait indefinitely.
+  WaitBeforePreemptionSeconds *int64
+
+  // <UNRESOLVED>
+
+  // allowPreemptionByLowerPriority determines whether pending Workloads can
+  // preempt Workloads with higher priority in this ClusterQueue.
```

  Correct, this is referring to high priority workloads running on borrowed resources.

+- [Design Details](#design-details)
+  - [Test Plan](#test-plan)
+      - [Prerequisite testing updates](#prerequisite-testing-updates)
+    - [Unit Tests](#unit-tests)
+    - [Integration tests](#integration-tests)
+  - [Graduation Criteria](#graduation-criteria)
+- [Implementation History](#implementation-history)
+- [Drawbacks](#drawbacks)
+- [Alternatives](#alternatives)
+<!-- /toc -->
+
+## Summary
+
+This enhancement introduces workload preemption, a mechanism to terminate
+workloads in ClusterQueues that are borrowing resources from the cohort
+when other ClusterQueues under their minimum quota need the resources.
+
+API fields in the Workload, LocalQueue and ClusterQueue objects control
+different preemption policies.
+
+## Motivation
+
+when ClusterQueues under their minimum quota lend resources, they should
+be able to recover those resources fast, to be able to admit workloads
+when there are sudden spikes.
+
+Currently, the only mechanism to recover those resources is to wait for
+Workloads to finish, which is generally unbounded.
+
+### Goals
+
+- Preempt Workloads from ClusterQueues borrowing resources when other
+  ClusterQueues in the cohort, under their minimum quota, need the resources.
+- Introduce API fields in Workload, LocalQueue and ClusterQueue to inform
+  which Workloads can be preempted and in which order.
+
+### Non-Goals
+
+- Graceful termination of Workloads is left to the controller that integrates
+  with the particular workload API.
+  For example, the integration with Job uses the [suspend field](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/job/#suspending-a-job).
+- Partial workload suspension is not supported.
+
+## Proposal
+
+<!--
+This is where we get down to the specifics of what the proposal actually is.
+This should have enough detail that reviewers can understand exactly what
+you're proposing, but should not include things like API designs or
+implementation. What is the desired outcome and how do we measure success?.
+The "Design Details" section below is for the real
+nitty-gritty.
+-->
+This enhancement proposes the introduction of the following APIs to influence
+Workload preemption:
+- A field in the Workload to describe the cost of preempting a Workload at a
+  given time.
+- A field in the LocalQueue to set Workload defaults.
+- A field in the ClusterQueue to influence how long to wait before starting
+  preemption.
+
+And an algorithm for selecting a set of Workloads using borrowed quota to be
+preempted when a Workload in a ClusterQueue is under the min quota.
+
+### User Stories (Optional)
+
+<!--
+Detail the things that people will be able to do if this KEP is implemented.
+Include as much detail as possible so that people can understand the "how" of
+the system. The goal here is to make this feel real for users without getting
+bogged down.
+-->
+
+#### Story 1
+
+As a batch user, I know how safe it is to disrupt some workloads after they
+have been running for some time.
+
+An sample Workload has the following characteristics:
+Early in their execution, the Workload hasn't made much progress, so it can be
+preempted. Later, the Worload is on the path of doing significant progress, so
+it's best not to disturb it. Lastly, the Workload is expected to have made
+some checkpoints, so it's ok to disturb it.
+
+This can be expressed with the following configuration:
+
+```yaml
+apiVersion: kueue.x-k8s.io/v1alpha2
+kind: Workload
+metadata:
+  name: my-workload
+spec:
+  preemption:
+    disruptionCostMilestones:
+    - seconds: 60
+      cost: 100
+    - seconds: 600
+      cost: 0
+```
```

  I learned this acronym/principle from Clayton the other day, YAGNI :)
  
  I am concerned that we are adding an API that will not be used, so I suggest we start simple and drop this API. We fix the ranking by priority then runtime (based on buckets like we did for replicasets downscaling). We can always add the API later if someone comes with a solid use case that helps inform how the API should look like.

@@ -0,0 +1,449 @@

+- [Implementation History](#implementation-history)
+- [Drawbacks](#drawbacks)
+- [Alternatives](#alternatives)
+<!-- /toc -->
+
+## Summary
+
+This enhancement introduces workload preemption, a mechanism to terminate
+workloads in ClusterQueues that are borrowing resources from the cohort
+when other ClusterQueues under their minimum quota need the resources.
+
+API fields in the Workload, LocalQueue and ClusterQueue objects control
+different preemption policies.
+
+## Motivation
+
+when ClusterQueues under their minimum quota lend resources, they should
+be able to recover those resources fast, to be able to admit workloads
+when there are sudden spikes.
+
+Currently, the only mechanism to recover those resources is to wait for
+Workloads to finish, which is generally unbounded.
+
+### Goals
+
+- Preempt Workloads from ClusterQueues borrowing resources when other
+  ClusterQueues in the cohort, under their minimum quota, need the resources.
+- Introduce API fields in Workload, LocalQueue and ClusterQueue to inform
+  which Workloads can be preempted and in which order.
+
+### Non-Goals
+
+- Graceful termination of Workloads is left to the controller that integrates
+  with the particular workload API.
+  For example, the integration with Job uses the [suspend field](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/job/#suspending-a-job).
+- Partial workload suspension is not supported.
+
+## Proposal
+
+<!--
+This is where we get down to the specifics of what the proposal actually is.
+This should have enough detail that reviewers can understand exactly what
+you're proposing, but should not include things like API designs or
+implementation. What is the desired outcome and how do we measure success?.
+The "Design Details" section below is for the real
+nitty-gritty.
+-->
+This enhancement proposes the introduction of the following APIs to influence
+Workload preemption:
+- A field in the Workload to describe the cost of preempting a Workload at a
+  given time.
+- A field in the LocalQueue to set Workload defaults.
+- A field in the ClusterQueue to influence how long to wait before starting
+  preemption.
+
+And an algorithm for selecting a set of Workloads using borrowed quota to be
+preempted when a Workload in a ClusterQueue is under the min quota.
+
+### User Stories (Optional)
+
+<!--
+Detail the things that people will be able to do if this KEP is implemented.
+Include as much detail as possible so that people can understand the "how" of
+the system. The goal here is to make this feel real for users without getting
+bogged down.
+-->
+
+#### Story 1
+
+As a batch user, I know how safe it is to disrupt some workloads after they
+have been running for some time.
+
+An sample Workload has the following characteristics:
+Early in their execution, the Workload hasn't made much progress, so it can be
+preempted. Later, the Worload is on the path of doing significant progress, so
+it's best not to disturb it. Lastly, the Workload is expected to have made
+some checkpoints, so it's ok to disturb it.
+
+This can be expressed with the following configuration:
+
+```yaml
+apiVersion: kueue.x-k8s.io/v1alpha2
+kind: Workload
+metadata:
+  name: my-workload
+spec:
+  preemption:
+    disruptionCostMilestones:
+    - seconds: 60
+      cost: 100
+    - seconds: 600
+      cost: 0
+```
```

  One thing is that I'm not sure if Batch Users should have that much control about the cost of preemption vs others, and they don't have much incentive to tune disruptionCostMilestones correctly. They might rather be tempted to inflate cost to reduce preemptions. Unless it's only within their jobs (but then we shouldn't compare cost between LocalQueues). Cluster Admin needs to control the fair rules.
  
  And +1 that disruptionCostMilestones feels quite artificial and too complex for a start. I think admins & users will think in different/simpler terms:
  - user: I just want results ASAP. If I see a lot of preemptions, I will implement checkpointing to help the job complete quicker. 
  - admin: I need to optimize fairness and minimize the amount of core hours lost.
  
  So +1 to start with a simple [ByLowestPriority, ByLowestRuntime] and gather some feedback from users.

- **keps/83-workload-preemption/README.md:94** by [@qelo](https://github.com/qelo) — 2022-10-13T13:06:17Z
```diff
@@ -0,0 +1,498 @@
+# KEP-83: Workload preemption
+
+<!-- toc -->
+- [Summary](#summary)
+- [Motivation](#motivation)
+  - [Goals](#goals)
+  - [Non-Goals](#non-goals)
+- [Proposal](#proposal)
+  - [User Stories (Optional)](#user-stories-optional)
+    - [Story 1](#story-1)
+    - [Story 2](#story-2)
+  - [Notes/Contraints/Caveats (Optional)](#notescontraintscaveats-optional)
+  - [Risks and Mitigations](#risks-and-mitigations)
+- [Design Details](#design-details)
+  - [Test Plan](#test-plan)
+      - [Prerequisite testing updates](#prerequisite-testing-updates)
+    - [Unit Tests](#unit-tests)
+    - [Integration tests](#integration-tests)
+  - [Graduation Criteria](#graduation-criteria)
+- [Implementation History](#implementation-history)
+- [Drawbacks](#drawbacks)
+- [Alternatives](#alternatives)
+<!-- /toc -->
+
+## Summary
+
+This enhancement introduces workload preemption, a mechanism to suspend
+workloads when:
+- ClusterQueues under their minimum quota need the resources that are currently
+  lent to other ClusterQueues in the cohort.
+- Within a Clusterqueue, there are running Workloads with lower priority than
+  a pending Workload.
+
+API fields in the Workload, LocalQueue and ClusterQueue objects control
+different preemption policies.
+
+## Motivation
+
+When ClusterQueues under their minimum quota lend resources, they should
+be able to recover those resources fast, to be able to admit Workloads
+when there are sudden spikes. Similarly, the ClusterQueue should be able to
+recover quota from low priority workloads that are currently running.
+
+Currently, the only mechanism to recover those resources is to wait for
+Workloads to finish, which is generally unbounded.
+
+### Goals
+
+- Preempt Workloads from ClusterQueues borrowing resources when other
+  ClusterQueues in the cohort, under their minimum quota, need the resources.
+- Preempt Workloads within a ClusterQueue when a high priority Workload doesn't
+  fit in the available quota, independently of borrowed quota.
+- Introduce API fields in Workload, LocalQueue and ClusterQueue to inform
+  which Workloads can be preempted and in which order.
+
+### Non-Goals
+
+- Graceful termination of Workloads is left to the workload pods to implement.
+- Tracking usage by workloads that take arbitrary time to be suspended. See
+  [Workload preemption doesn't imply immediate Pod termination](#workload-preemption-doesnt-imply-immediate-pod-termination) to learn more.
+  For example, the integration with Job uses the [suspend field](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/job/#suspending-a-job).
+- Partial workload suspension is not supported.
+
+## Proposal
+
+<!--
+This is where we get down to the specifics of what the proposal actually is.
+This should have enough detail that reviewers can understand exactly what
+you're proposing, but should not include things like API designs or
+implementation. What is the desired outcome and how do we measure success?.
+The "Design Details" section below is for the real
+nitty-gritty.
+-->
+This enhancement proposes the introduction of the following APIs to influence
+Workload preemption:
+- A field in the Workload to describe the cost of preempting a Workload at a
+  given time.
+- A field in the LocalQueue to set Workload defaults.
+- A field in the ClusterQueue to influence how long to wait before starting
+  preemption.
+
+And an algorithm for selecting a set of Workloads using borrowed quota to be
+preempted when a Workload in a ClusterQueue is under the min quota.
```

  What happens to preempted workloads, do they re-enter the queue or finish as failed?

### Review by [@alculquicondor](https://github.com/alculquicondor) (commented) — 2022-10-13T15:52:43Z

- **keps/83-workload-preemption/README.md:112** by [@alculquicondor](https://github.com/alculquicondor) — 2022-10-13T15:52:43Z
```diff
@@ -0,0 +1,449 @@
+# KEP-83: Workload preemption
+
+<!-- toc -->
+- [Summary](#summary)
+- [Motivation](#motivation)
+  - [Goals](#goals)
+  - [Non-Goals](#non-goals)
+- [Proposal](#proposal)
+  - [User Stories (Optional)](#user-stories-optional)
+    - [Story 1](#story-1)
+    - [Story 2](#story-2)
+  - [Notes/Contraints/Caveats (Optional)](#notescontraintscaveats-optional)
+  - [Risks and Mitigations](#risks-and-mitigations)
+- [Design Details](#design-details)
+  - [Test Plan](#test-plan)
+      - [Prerequisite testing updates](#prerequisite-testing-updates)
+    - [Unit Tests](#unit-tests)
+    - [Integration tests](#integration-tests)
+  - [Graduation Criteria](#graduation-criteria)
+- [Implementation History](#implementation-history)
+- [Drawbacks](#drawbacks)
+- [Alternatives](#alternatives)
+<!-- /toc -->
+
+## Summary
+
+This enhancement introduces workload preemption, a mechanism to terminate
+workloads in ClusterQueues that are borrowing resources from the cohort
+when other ClusterQueues under their minimum quota need the resources.
+
+API fields in the Workload, LocalQueue and ClusterQueue objects control
+different preemption policies.
+
+## Motivation
+
+when ClusterQueues under their minimum quota lend resources, they should
+be able to recover those resources fast, to be able to admit workloads
+when there are sudden spikes.
+
+Currently, the only mechanism to recover those resources is to wait for
+Workloads to finish, which is generally unbounded.
+
+### Goals
+
+- Preempt Workloads from ClusterQueues borrowing resources when other
+  ClusterQueues in the cohort, under their minimum quota, need the resources.
+- Introduce API fields in Workload, LocalQueue and ClusterQueue to inform
+  which Workloads can be preempted and in which order.
+
+### Non-Goals
+
+- Graceful termination of Workloads is left to the controller that integrates
+  with the particular workload API.
+  For example, the integration with Job uses the [suspend field](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/job/#suspending-a-job).
+- Partial workload suspension is not supported.
+
+## Proposal
+
+<!--
+This is where we get down to the specifics of what the proposal actually is.
+This should have enough detail that reviewers can understand exactly what
+you're proposing, but should not include things like API designs or
+implementation. What is the desired outcome and how do we measure success?.
+The "Design Details" section below is for the real
+nitty-gritty.
+-->
+This enhancement proposes the introduction of the following APIs to influence
+Workload preemption:
+- A field in the Workload to describe the cost of preempting a Workload at a
+  given time.
+- A field in the LocalQueue to set Workload defaults.
+- A field in the ClusterQueue to influence how long to wait before starting
+  preemption.
+
+And an algorithm for selecting a set of Workloads using borrowed quota to be
+preempted when a Workload in a ClusterQueue is under the min quota.
+
+### User Stories (Optional)
+
+<!--
+Detail the things that people will be able to do if this KEP is implemented.
+Include as much detail as possible so that people can understand the "how" of
+the system. The goal here is to make this feel real for users without getting
+bogged down.
+-->
+
+#### Story 1
+
+As a batch user, I know how safe it is to disrupt some workloads after they
+have been running for some time.
+
+An sample Workload has the following characteristics:
+Early in their execution, the Workload hasn't made much progress, so it can be
+preempted. Later, the Worload is on the path of doing significant progress, so
+it's best not to disturb it. Lastly, the Workload is expected to have made
+some checkpoints, so it's ok to disturb it.
+
+This can be expressed with the following configuration:
+
+```yaml
+apiVersion: kueue.x-k8s.io/v1alpha2
+kind: Workload
+metadata:
+  name: my-workload
+spec:
+  preemption:
+    disruptionCostMilestones:
+    - seconds: 60
+      cost: 100
+    - seconds: 600
+      cost: 0
+```
```

  I guess we can say that the request in https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/kueue/issues/83#issuecomment-1224602577 is mostly covered by the second story and drop this for later.

### Review by [@alculquicondor](https://github.com/alculquicondor) (commented) — 2022-10-14T18:53:25Z

@@ -0,0 +1,498 @@
+# KEP-83: Workload preemption
+
+<!-- toc -->
+- [Summary](#summary)
+- [Motivation](#motivation)
+  - [Goals](#goals)
+  - [Non-Goals](#non-goals)
+- [Proposal](#proposal)
+  - [User Stories (Optional)](#user-stories-optional)
+    - [Story 1](#story-1)
+    - [Story 2](#story-2)
+  - [Notes/Contraints/Caveats (Optional)](#notescontraintscaveats-optional)
+  - [Risks and Mitigations](#risks-and-mitigations)
+- [Design Details](#design-details)
+  - [Test Plan](#test-plan)
+      - [Prerequisite testing updates](#prerequisite-testing-updates)
+    - [Unit Tests](#unit-tests)
+    - [Integration tests](#integration-tests)
+  - [Graduation Criteria](#graduation-criteria)
+- [Implementation History](#implementation-history)
+- [Drawbacks](#drawbacks)
+- [Alternatives](#alternatives)
+<!-- /toc -->
+
+## Summary
+
+This enhancement introduces workload preemption, a mechanism to suspend
+workloads when:
+- ClusterQueues under their minimum quota need the resources that are currently
+  lent to other ClusterQueues in the cohort.
+- Within a Clusterqueue, there are running Workloads with lower priority than
+  a pending Workload.
+
+API fields in the Workload, LocalQueue and ClusterQueue objects control
+different preemption policies.
+
+## Motivation
+
+When ClusterQueues under their minimum quota lend resources, they should
+be able to recover those resources fast, to be able to admit Workloads
+when there are sudden spikes. Similarly, the ClusterQueue should be able to
+recover quota from low priority workloads that are currently running.
+
+Currently, the only mechanism to recover those resources is to wait for
+Workloads to finish, which is generally unbounded.
+
+### Goals
+
+- Preempt Workloads from ClusterQueues borrowing resources when other
+  ClusterQueues in the cohort, under their minimum quota, need the resources.
+- Preempt Workloads within a ClusterQueue when a high priority Workload doesn't
+  fit in the available quota, independently of borrowed quota.
+- Introduce API fields in Workload, LocalQueue and ClusterQueue to inform
+  which Workloads can be preempted and in which order.
+
+### Non-Goals
+
+- Graceful termination of Workloads is left to the workload pods to implement.
+- Tracking usage by workloads that take arbitrary time to be suspended. See
+  [Workload preemption doesn't imply immediate Pod termination](#workload-preemption-doesnt-imply-immediate-pod-termination) to learn more.
+  For example, the integration with Job uses the [suspend field](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/job/#suspending-a-job).
+- Partial workload suspension is not supported.
+
+## Proposal
+
+<!--
+This is where we get down to the specifics of what the proposal actually is.
+This should have enough detail that reviewers can understand exactly what
+you're proposing, but should not include things like API designs or
+implementation. What is the desired outcome and how do we measure success?.
+The "Design Details" section below is for the real
+nitty-gritty.
+-->
+This enhancement proposes the introduction of the following APIs to influence
+Workload preemption:
+- A field in the Workload to describe the cost of preempting a Workload at a
+  given time.
+- A field in the LocalQueue to set Workload defaults.
+- A field in the ClusterQueue to influence how long to wait before starting
+  preemption.
+
+And an algorithm for selecting a set of Workloads using borrowed quota to be
+preempted when a Workload in a ClusterQueue is under the min quota.
```

  Added as a knob

- **keps/83-workload-preemption/README.md:299** by [@alculquicondor](https://github.com/alculquicondor) — 2022-10-14T18:17:33Z
```diff
@@ -0,0 +1,449 @@
+# KEP-83: Workload preemption
+
+<!-- toc -->
+- [Summary](#summary)
+- [Motivation](#motivation)
+  - [Goals](#goals)
+  - [Non-Goals](#non-goals)
+- [Proposal](#proposal)
+  - [User Stories (Optional)](#user-stories-optional)
+    - [Story 1](#story-1)
+    - [Story 2](#story-2)
+  - [Notes/Contraints/Caveats (Optional)](#notescontraintscaveats-optional)
+  - [Risks and Mitigations](#risks-and-mitigations)
+- [Design Details](#design-details)
+  - [Test Plan](#test-plan)
+      - [Prerequisite testing updates](#prerequisite-testing-updates)
+    - [Unit Tests](#unit-tests)
+    - [Integration tests](#integration-tests)
+  - [Graduation Criteria](#graduation-criteria)
+- [Implementation History](#implementation-history)
+- [Drawbacks](#drawbacks)
+- [Alternatives](#alternatives)
+<!-- /toc -->
+
+## Summary
+
+This enhancement introduces workload preemption, a mechanism to terminate
+workloads in ClusterQueues that are borrowing resources from the cohort
+when other ClusterQueues under their minimum quota need the resources.
+
+API fields in the Workload, LocalQueue and ClusterQueue objects control
+different preemption policies.
+
+## Motivation
+
+when ClusterQueues under their minimum quota lend resources, they should
+be able to recover those resources fast, to be able to admit workloads
+when there are sudden spikes.
+
+Currently, the only mechanism to recover those resources is to wait for
+Workloads to finish, which is generally unbounded.
+
+### Goals
+
+- Preempt Workloads from ClusterQueues borrowing resources when other
+  ClusterQueues in the cohort, under their minimum quota, need the resources.
+- Introduce API fields in Workload, LocalQueue and ClusterQueue to inform
+  which Workloads can be preempted and in which order.
+
+### Non-Goals
+
+- Graceful termination of Workloads is left to the controller that integrates
+  with the particular workload API.
+  For example, the integration with Job uses the [suspend field](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/job/#suspending-a-job).
+- Partial workload suspension is not supported.
+
+## Proposal
+
+<!--
+This is where we get down to the specifics of what the proposal actually is.
+This should have enough detail that reviewers can understand exactly what
+you're proposing, but should not include things like API designs or
+implementation. What is the desired outcome and how do we measure success?.
+The "Design Details" section below is for the real
+nitty-gritty.
+-->
+This enhancement proposes the introduction of the following APIs to influence
+Workload preemption:
+- A field in the Workload to describe the cost of preempting a Workload at a
+  given time.
+- A field in the LocalQueue to set Workload defaults.
+- A field in the ClusterQueue to influence how long to wait before starting
+  preemption.
+
+And an algorithm for selecting a set of Workloads using borrowed quota to be
+preempted when a Workload in a ClusterQueue is under the min quota.
+
+### User Stories (Optional)
+
+<!--
+Detail the things that people will be able to do if this KEP is implemented.
+Include as much detail as possible so that people can understand the "how" of
+the system. The goal here is to make this feel real for users without getting
+bogged down.
+-->
+
+#### Story 1
+
+As a batch user, I know how safe it is to disrupt some workloads after they
+have been running for some time.
+
+An sample Workload has the following characteristics:
+Early in their execution, the Workload hasn't made much progress, so it can be
+preempted. Later, the Worload is on the path of doing significant progress, so
+it's best not to disturb it. Lastly, the Workload is expected to have made
+some checkpoints, so it's ok to disturb it.
+
+This can be expressed with the following configuration:
+
+```yaml
+apiVersion: kueue.x-k8s.io/v1alpha2
+kind: Workload
+metadata:
+  name: my-workload
+spec:
+  preemption:
+    disruptionCostMilestones:
+    - seconds: 60
+      cost: 100
+    - seconds: 600
+      cost: 0
+```
+
+The cost is a linear interpolation of the configuration above. A graphical
+representation of the cost looks like the following (not to scale):
+
+```
+cost
+
+100      __
+        /  \___
+       /       \___
+      /            \___
+0    /                 \_
+    0    60             600  time
+```
+
+As a cluster administrator, I can configure default `disruptionCostMilestones`
+for certain workloads using webhooks or setting them for all Workloads in a
+LocalQueue:
+
+```yaml
+apiVersion: kueue.x-k8s.io/v1alpha2
+kind: LocalQueue
+metadata:
+  name: cluster-total
+spec:
+  preemptionDefaults:
+    disruptionCostMilestones:
+    - seconds: 60
+      cost: 100
+    - seconds: 600
+      cost: 0
+```
+
+#### Story 2
+
+As a cluster administrator, in the cluster I have cohorts with different
+characteristics:
+
+- In some cohorts, all the ClusterQueues are known to have short-running
+  workloads that don't do checkpointing. In this case, it's fine to wait for
+  jobs to finish, but don't allow ClusterQueues to borrow more resources.
+- In other cohorts, ClusterQueues might have long running jobs that support
+  checkpointing. In this case, it's necessary to preempt Workloads that are
+  using borrowed quota.
+
+I want to configure in the ClusterQueue how long to wait for Workloads to finish
+up to a limit.
+
+```yaml
+apiVersion: kueue.x-k8s.io/v1alpha2
+kind: ClusterQueue
+metadata:
+  name: cluster-total
+spec:
+  preemption:
+    waitBeforePreemptionSeconds: 600 # leave empty to wait indefinitely.
+```
+
+### Notes/Contraints/Caveats (Optional)
+
+<!--
+What are the caveats to the proposal?
+What are some important details that didn't come across above?
+Go in to as much detail as necessary here.
+This might be a good place to talk about core concepts and how they relate.
+-->
+
+### Risks and Mitigations
+
+<!--
+What are the risks of this proposal, and how do we mitigate? Think broadly.
+For example, consider both security and how this will impact the larger
+Kubernetes ecosystem.
+
+How will security be reviewed, and by whom?
+
+How will UX be reviewed, and by whom?
+
+Consider including folks who also work outside the SIG or subproject.
+-->
+### Increased admission latency
+
+## Design Details
+
+The proposal consists of new API fields and a preemption algorithm.
+
+### API
+
+#### Workload changes
+
+The new API fields in a Workload describe the policies and preferences for
+preempting the Workload.
+
+```golang
+
+const WorkloadStarted = "Started"
+
+type WorkloadSpec struct {
+  ...
+  // preemption describes the policies and preferences for preempting this
+  // Workload.
+  // preemption is immutable once set.
+  Preemption *WorkloadPreemption
+}
+
+type WorklodPreemption struct {
+  // disruptionCostMilestones defines a cost curve for preempting this workload
+  // based on the time the workload has been running.
+  // The curve is defined by points of (time, cost). The cost is calculated from
+  // a linear interpolation of the cost between the two time points that contain
+  // the current workload runtime.
+  // The first point is implicitly (0, 0)
+  // The cost from the last milestone is constant.
+  //
+  // For example: given the points (60, 0) and (120, 100), the cost of
+  // preempting the Workload after it has been running for:
+  // - 30s is 0,
+  // - 90s is 50,
+  // - 300s is 100.
+  //
+  // disruptionCostMilestones[i].time > disruptioncostMilestones[j].time for i > j
+  DisruptionCostMilestones []DisruptionCostMilestone.
+}
+
+type DisruptionCostMilestone struct {
+  // seconds is the time since the Workload started (timestamp of the Started
+  // condition on the Workload).
+  Seconds int64
+
+  // cost is an integer representation of the cost of disrupting the Workload
+  // at a given time. The value has meaning when compared to the cost of
+  // disrupting other Workloads.
+  //
+  // 0 <= cost <= 100
+  Cost int32
+}
+```
+
+#### LocalQueue changes
+
+The new API fields in LocalQueue describe the defaults that apply to Workloads
+in this LocalQueue.
+
+```golang
+type LocalQueueSpec struct {
+  ...
+  // preemptionDefaults are the defaults applied to Workloads assigned to this
+  // LocalQueue. The defauts are applied to Workloads at the time of the
+  // Workload admission.
+  PreemptionDefaults *WorkloadPreemption
+}
+```
+
+#### ClusterQueue changes
+
+The new API fields in ClusterQueue describe how to influence the selection
+of Workloads to preempt.
+
+```golang
+const ClusterQueuePendingWorkloadsUnderMinQuota = "PendingWorkloadsUnderMinQuota"
+
+type ClusterQueueSpec struct {
+  ...
+  // preemption describes policies to preempt Workloads from ClusterQueues
+  // borrowing resources when this ClusterQueue has pending Workloads and the
+  // min quota is unmet.
+  preemption ClusterQueuePreemption
+}
+
+type ClusterQueueStatus struct {
+  ...
+  conditions []metav1.Condition
+}
+
+type ClusterQueuePreemption struct {
+  // waitBeforePreemptionSeconds is the time in seconds that the ClusterQueue
+  // is willing to wait for Workloads to finish before issuing preemptions,
+  // since the first time that the ClusterQueue has pending workloads that would
+  // fit under the min quota. This time is present in the timestamp for the
+  // PendingWorkloadsUnderMinQuota condition.
+  // If null, it means to wait indefinitely.
+  WaitBeforePreemptionSeconds *int64
+
+  // <UNRESOLVED>
+
+  // allowPreemptionByLowerPriority determines whether pending Workloads can
+  // preempt Workloads with higher priority in this ClusterQueue.
```

  Renaming to `AllowPreemptionByLowerPriorityWhenBorrowing` :)
  
  Should we add this API? In a way, I'm thinking that high priority jobs should never run on borrowed capacity, which can be configured via quotas.
  
  But an argument could be made that a high priority job probably should be able to use all the capacity available in a cluster.
  
  Although, in both cases, it sounds like the default should be false and we can leave that as an implicit behavior for now.

- **keps/83-workload-preemption/README.md:112** by [@alculquicondor](https://github.com/alculquicondor) — 2022-10-14T18:53:19Z

+
+## Summary
+
+This enhancement introduces workload preemption, a mechanism to terminate
+workloads in ClusterQueues that are borrowing resources from the cohort
+when other ClusterQueues under their minimum quota need the resources.
+
+API fields in the Workload, LocalQueue and ClusterQueue objects control
+different preemption policies.
+
+## Motivation
+
+when ClusterQueues under their minimum quota lend resources, they should
+be able to recover those resources fast, to be able to admit workloads
+when there are sudden spikes.
+
+Currently, the only mechanism to recover those resources is to wait for
+Workloads to finish, which is generally unbounded.
+
+### Goals
+
+- Preempt Workloads from ClusterQueues borrowing resources when other
+  ClusterQueues in the cohort, under their minimum quota, need the resources.
+- Introduce API fields in Workload, LocalQueue and ClusterQueue to inform
+  which Workloads can be preempted and in which order.
+
+### Non-Goals
+
+- Graceful termination of Workloads is left to the controller that integrates
+  with the particular workload API.
+  For example, the integration with Job uses the [suspend field](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/job/#suspending-a-job).
+- Partial workload suspension is not supported.
+
+## Proposal
+
+<!--
+This is where we get down to the specifics of what the proposal actually is.
+This should have enough detail that reviewers can understand exactly what
+you're proposing, but should not include things like API designs or
+implementation. What is the desired outcome and how do we measure success?.
+The "Design Details" section below is for the real
+nitty-gritty.
+-->
+This enhancement proposes the introduction of the following APIs to influence
+Workload preemption:
+- A field in the Workload to describe the cost of preempting a Workload at a
+  given time.
+- A field in the LocalQueue to set Workload defaults.
+- A field in the ClusterQueue to influence how long to wait before starting
+  preemption.
+
+And an algorithm for selecting a set of Workloads using borrowed quota to be
+preempted when a Workload in a ClusterQueue is under the min quota.
+
+### User Stories (Optional)
+
+<!--
+Detail the things that people will be able to do if this KEP is implemented.
+Include as much detail as possible so that people can understand the "how" of
+the system. The goal here is to make this feel real for users without getting
+bogged down.
+-->
+
+#### Story 1
+
+As a batch user, I know how safe it is to disrupt some workloads after they
+have been running for some time.
+
+An sample Workload has the following characteristics:
+Early in their execution, the Workload hasn't made much progress, so it can be
+preempted. Later, the Worload is on the path of doing significant progress, so
+it's best not to disturb it. Lastly, the Workload is expected to have made
+some checkpoints, so it's ok to disturb it.
+
+This can be expressed with the following configuration:
+
+```yaml
+apiVersion: kueue.x-k8s.io/v1alpha2
+kind: Workload
+metadata:
+  name: my-workload
+spec:
+  preemption:
+    disruptionCostMilestones:
+    - seconds: 60
+      cost: 100
+    - seconds: 600
+      cost: 0
+```
```

  Moved to Alternatives

### Review by [@kerthcet](https://github.com/kerthcet) (commented) — 2022-10-17T14:31:17Z

- **keps/83-workload-preemption/README.md:85** by [@kerthcet](https://github.com/kerthcet) — 2022-10-17T09:47:27Z

@@ -0,0 +1,474 @@
+# KEP-83: Workload preemption
+
+<!-- toc -->
+- [Summary](#summary)

+- [Proposal](#proposal)

+    - [Story 1](#story-1)
+    - [Story 2](#story-2)
+  - [Notes/Contraints/Caveats (Optional)](#notescontraintscaveats-optional)
+    - [How to configure waitBeforePreemptionSeconds](#how-to-configure-waitbeforepreemptionseconds)
+    - [Workload preemption doesn't imply immediate Pod termination](#workload-preemption-doesnt-imply-immediate-pod-termination)
+    - [Why no control to opt-out a ClusterQueue from preemption](#why-no-control-to-opt-out-a-clusterqueue-from-preemption)
+  - [Risks and Mitigations](#risks-and-mitigations)
+  - [Increased admission latency](#increased-admission-latency)
+- [Design Details](#design-details)
+  - [API](#api)
+    - [ClusterQueue changes](#clusterqueue-changes)
+  - [Workload changes](#workload-changes)
+  - [Preemption algorithm](#preemption-algorithm)
+    - [Detecting Workloads that need lent resources](#detecting-workloads-that-need-lent-resources)
+  - [Test Plan](#test-plan)
+      - [Prerequisite testing updates](#prerequisite-testing-updates)
+    - [Unit Tests](#unit-tests)
+    - [Integration tests](#integration-tests)
+  - [Graduation Criteria](#graduation-criteria)
+- [Implementation History](#implementation-history)
+- [Drawbacks](#drawbacks)
+- [Alternatives](#alternatives)
+  - [Inform how costly is to interrupt a Workload](#inform-how-costly-is-to-interrupt-a-workload)
+  - [Extra knobs in ClusterQueue preemption policy](#extra-knobs-in-clusterqueue-preemption-policy)
+<!-- /toc -->
+
+## Summary
+
+This enhancement introduces workload preemption, a mechanism to suspend
+workloads when:
+- ClusterQueues under their minimum quota need the resources that are currently
+  lent to other ClusterQueues in the cohort.
+- Within a Clusterqueue, there are running Workloads with lower priority than
+  a pending Workload.
+
+API fields in the Workload and ClusterQueue definitions determine preemption policies.
+
+## Motivation
+
+When ClusterQueues under their minimum quota lend resources, they should
+be able to recover those resources fast, to be able to admit Workloads
+when there are sudden spikes. Similarly, the ClusterQueue should be able to
+recover quota from low priority workloads that are currently running.
+
+Currently, the only mechanism to recover those resources is to wait for
+Workloads to finish, which is generally unbounded.
+
+### Goals
+
+- Preempt Workloads from ClusterQueues borrowing resources when other
+  ClusterQueues in the cohort, under their minimum quota, need the resources.
+- Preempt Workloads within a ClusterQueue when a high priority Workload doesn't
+  fit in the available quota, independently of borrowed quota.
+- Introduce API fields in ClusterQueue to control when preemption occurs.
+
+### Non-Goals
+
+- Graceful termination of Workloads is left to the workload pods to implement.
+- Tracking usage by workloads that take arbitrary time to be suspended. See
+  [Workload preemption doesn't imply immediate Pod termination](#workload-preemption-doesnt-imply-immediate-pod-termination) to learn more.
+  For example, the integration with Job uses the [suspend field](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/job/#suspending-a-job).
+- Partial workload suspension is not supported.
+
+## Proposal
+
+<!--
+This is where we get down to the specifics of what the proposal actually is.
+This should have enough detail that reviewers can understand exactly what
+you're proposing, but should not include things like API designs or
+implementation. What is the desired outcome and how do we measure success?.
+The "Design Details" section below is for the real
+nitty-gritty.
+-->
+This enhancement proposes the introduction of the following APIs to influence
+Workload preemption:
+- A field in the ClusterQueue to influence how long to wait before starting
```

  Why not add this to the localQueue, clusterQueue used for resource sharing between different localQueues, then different localQueues may have different timeouts. 
  
  Or this is a followup with clusterQueue timeout, when localQueue doesn't have a timeout, it will apply the clusterQueue's.

- **keps/83-workload-preemption/README.md:127** by [@kerthcet](https://github.com/kerthcet) — 2022-10-17T10:07:38Z

@@ -0,0 +1,474 @@
+# KEP-83: Workload preemption
+
+<!-- toc -->

+    - [Why no control to opt-out a ClusterQueue from preemption](#why-no-control-to-opt-out-a-clusterqueue-from-preemption)
+  - [Risks and Mitigations](#risks-and-mitigations)
+  - [Increased admission latency](#increased-admission-latency)
+- [Design Details](#design-details)
+  - [API](#api)
+    - [ClusterQueue changes](#clusterqueue-changes)
+  - [Workload changes](#workload-changes)
+  - [Preemption algorithm](#preemption-algorithm)
+    - [Detecting Workloads that need lent resources](#detecting-workloads-that-need-lent-resources)
+  - [Test Plan](#test-plan)
+      - [Prerequisite testing updates](#prerequisite-testing-updates)
+    - [Unit Tests](#unit-tests)
+    - [Integration tests](#integration-tests)
+  - [Graduation Criteria](#graduation-criteria)
+- [Implementation History](#implementation-history)
+- [Drawbacks](#drawbacks)
+- [Alternatives](#alternatives)
+  - [Inform how costly is to interrupt a Workload](#inform-how-costly-is-to-interrupt-a-workload)
+  - [Extra knobs in ClusterQueue preemption policy](#extra-knobs-in-clusterqueue-preemption-policy)
+<!-- /toc -->
+
+## Summary
+
+This enhancement introduces workload preemption, a mechanism to suspend
+workloads when:
+- ClusterQueues under their minimum quota need the resources that are currently
+  lent to other ClusterQueues in the cohort.
+- Within a Clusterqueue, there are running Workloads with lower priority than
+  a pending Workload.
+
+API fields in the Workload and ClusterQueue definitions determine preemption policies.
+
+## Motivation
+
+When ClusterQueues under their minimum quota lend resources, they should
+be able to recover those resources fast, to be able to admit Workloads
+when there are sudden spikes. Similarly, the ClusterQueue should be able to
+recover quota from low priority workloads that are currently running.
+
+Currently, the only mechanism to recover those resources is to wait for
+Workloads to finish, which is generally unbounded.
+
+### Goals
+
+- Preempt Workloads from ClusterQueues borrowing resources when other
+  ClusterQueues in the cohort, under their minimum quota, need the resources.
+- Preempt Workloads within a ClusterQueue when a high priority Workload doesn't
+  fit in the available quota, independently of borrowed quota.
+- Introduce API fields in ClusterQueue to control when preemption occurs.
+
+### Non-Goals
+
+- Graceful termination of Workloads is left to the workload pods to implement.
+- Tracking usage by workloads that take arbitrary time to be suspended. See
+  [Workload preemption doesn't imply immediate Pod termination](#workload-preemption-doesnt-imply-immediate-pod-termination) to learn more.
+  For example, the integration with Job uses the [suspend field](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/job/#suspending-a-job).
+- Partial workload suspension is not supported.
+
+## Proposal
+
+<!--
+This is where we get down to the specifics of what the proposal actually is.
+This should have enough detail that reviewers can understand exactly what
+you're proposing, but should not include things like API designs or
+implementation. What is the desired outcome and how do we measure success?.
+The "Design Details" section below is for the real
+nitty-gritty.
+-->
+This enhancement proposes the introduction of the following APIs to influence
+Workload preemption:
+- A field in the ClusterQueue to influence how long to wait before starting
+  preemption.
+- A field in the Workload to determine whether to terminate or requeue upon preemption.
+
+And an algorithm for selecting a set of Workloads using borrowed quota to be
+preempted when a Workload in a ClusterQueue is under the min quota.
+
+### User Stories (Optional)
+
+<!--
+Detail the things that people will be able to do if this KEP is implemented.
+Include as much detail as possible so that people can understand the "how" of
+the system. The goal here is to make this feel real for users without getting
+bogged down.
+-->
+
+#### Story 1
+
+As a cluster administrator, in the cluster I have cohorts with different
+characteristics:
+
+- In some cohorts, all the ClusterQueues are known to have short-running
+  workloads that don't do checkpointing. In this case, it's fine to wait for
+  jobs to finish, but don't allow ClusterQueues to borrow more resources.
+- In other cohorts, ClusterQueues might have long running jobs that support
+  checkpointing. In this case, it's necessary to preempt Workloads that are
+  using borrowed quota.
+
+I want to configure in the ClusterQueue how long to wait for Workloads to finish
+up to a limit.
+
+```yaml
+apiVersion: kueue.x-k8s.io/v1alpha2
+kind: ClusterQueue
+metadata:
+  name: cluster-total
+spec:
+  preemption:
+    waitBeforePreemptionSeconds: 600 # leave empty to wait indefinitely.
```

  A discussion here: will API-Convention accepts a semantic value here, like 10s, 10m, 7d, here. Or do you think it's necessary? For some tainting jobs will continue for quite a long time. 604800s is less readability than 7d or 1w.

- **keps/83-workload-preemption/README.md:127** by [@kerthcet](https://github.com/kerthcet) — 2022-10-17T10:10:40Z
```diff
